atomic_claim: rst-workbench simplifies installation and usage of multiple end-to-end RST parsers.
rst-workbench provides a web-based interface that allows users to enter text and generate concurrent analyses from multiple RST parsers.
rst-workbench enables visual comparison, manual post-editing in the browser, and storage of generated RST trees.
SF-QA provides a modular evaluation framework for open-domain question answering.
SF-QA modularizes the pipeline of open-domain question answering systems.
SF-QA enables accessible and reproducible open-domain question answering evaluation for research groups with limited computing resources.
SF-QA is publicly released and supports community contributions to its code and evaluations.
SAFAR is a monolingual framework dedicated to Arabic language that supports modern standard Arabic and Moroccan dialect.
SAFAR integrates a large collection of Arabic NLP tools and resources.
SAFAR provides access to its components through an API and a web interface.
SAFAR was developed in accordance with software engineering requirements.
A method extracts personal relationships from dependency trees in natural language texts.
The method calculates surprise scores for distributed representations of extracted relationships in an unsupervised manner.
The method does not require any labeled dataset for surprising personal relationships.
Human evaluation shows the method extracts more interesting relationships between persons from Japanese Wikipedia articles than a popularity-based baseline.
The method is demonstrated as a Chrome plugin for Google search.
T2NER is a Transformers-based transfer learning framework for named entity recognition implemented in PyTorch.
T2NER supports sequential transfer, domain adaptation, multi-task learning, and semi-supervised learning for named entity recognition.
T2NER provides a unified, extensible platform for transfer learning research and real-world named entity recognition applications.
FrameForm is an open-source annotation tool for predicate annotations based on Frame Semantics.
FrameForm enables creation, annotation, and maintenance of computational lexicography projects such as FrameNet.
FrameForm was used to build the Turkish FrameNet.
FrameForm is responsive and can be easily modified to meet the annotation needs of a wide range of languages.
CovRelex retrieves scientific papers and extracts entities and relations from COVID-19 literature using relation extraction.
CovRelex enables efficient knowledge acquisition across a large collection of rapidly published COVID-19 scientific papers.
The dashboard visualizes COVID-19 case counts on an interactive map with a navigational panel.
The dashboard incorporates a curated catalog of COVID-19 facts and debunked misinformation.
The dashboard displays the most prevalent catalog information among Twitter users in user-selected U.S. geographic regions.
BERT models are used to match tweets with catalog facts and misinformation and to detect tweet stance toward that information.
Preliminary experiments analyze the spatio-temporal spread of COVID-19 misinformation on Twitter.
GCM generates synthetic code-mixed sentences from parallel bilingual data by implementing the Equivalence Constraint theory and the Matrix Language theory.
GCM samples generated sentences to produce natural code-mixed text.
GCM offers three operation modes: batch processing, interactive library usage, and a web interface.
GCM can be used to create unlabeled code-mixed text for training pre-trained models.
GCM visualizes linguistic theories of code-mixing.
ASAD provides dialect identification for Arabic tweets.
ASAD provides sentiment analysis for Arabic tweets.
ASAD provides news category classification for Arabic tweets.
ASAD provides offensiveness detection for Arabic tweets.
ASAD provides hate speech detection for Arabic tweets.
ASAD provides adult content detection for Arabic tweets.
ASAD provides spam detection for Arabic tweets.
ASAD is accessible via a web API and a web interface that accept text input or file uploads.
Fine-tuning a language model with policy gradient reinforcement learning directly optimizes for better generation.
Combining policy gradient reinforcement learning with unlikelihood training further reduces repetition in generated text without impacting language model quality.
Various methods for improving generation at training and decoding time are evaluated and compared using metrics that assess control over text generation output.
Explicit syntactic and semantic structures are incorporated into a non-autoregressive Transformer for neural machine translation.
Intermediate latent alignment within target sentences is used to better learn long-term token dependencies in non-autoregressive translation.
The enriched non-autoregressive Transformer achieves significantly faster decoding speed while maintaining translation quality compared to state-of-the-art non-autoregressive models.
An automatic speech translation system provides live subtitling of conference presentations.
A strategy integrates numerous individually laboratory-tested components into a complex system for end-users.
The prototype recognizes English, Czech, and German speech and simultaneously presents translations in 42 target languages.
In neural morphological inflection, the empty string is almost never the most probable solution under the model.
Greedy search often finds the global optimum in neural morphological inflection.
Poor calibration of neural sequence-to-sequence models is likely caused by characteristics of specific task subsets rather than a general unsuitability for language generation.
An open-source library enables efficient low-level processing of ten major South Asian Brahmic scripts.
The library provides a flexible and extensible framework for operations on Brahmic scripts, including NFC, visual normalization, reversible transliteration, and validity checks.
The library implements these operations in Python using a finite-state transducer formalism.
A survey of common Brahmic script issues that may adversely affect downstream NLP tasks is presented.
A rationale for employing a finite-state design for Brahmic script processing is provided.

Lexical paraphrases and high-precision rules informed by news discourse structure can be used to automatically collect coreferential and non-coreferential event pairs from unlabeled English news articles.
Manual validation and empirical evaluation on multiple datasets assess the quality of automatically acquired event pairs across different event domains and text genres.
A model trained on automatically acquired event pairs performs comparably to a supervised model when applied to new data outside the training domains.
Augmenting human-annotated data with automatically acquired event pairs yields performance gains on both in-domain and out-of-domain evaluation datasets.
COCO-EX extracts concepts from texts and links them to ConceptNet knowledge graph nodes.
COCO-EX utilizes the maximum relational information stored in ConceptNet.
COCO-EX handles non-canonicalized, free-form text nodes in ConceptNet, addressing issues of unnormalized, nested, and noisy concepts.
COCO-EX extracts more meaningful and important concepts than string matching.
COCO-EX enables assessment of more relational information in ConceptNet than string matching.
Training-test data leakage is identified in several publicly available NLP evaluation datasets, including named entity recognition and relation extraction datasets.
The impact of data leakage on model memorization versus generalization is quantitatively assessed.
Overlap between training and test sets is shown to inflate evaluation results, causing models to appear to generalize when they are memorizing.
SpanEmo casts multi-label emotion classification as a span-prediction task.
SpanEmo incorporates a loss function that models multiple co-existing emotions in a sentence.
SpanEmo improves performance on the SemEval2018 multi-label emotion dataset across English, Arabic, and Spanish.
SpanEmo learns meaningful associations between emotion classes and words in a sentence.
OCTIS provides a framework for training, analyzing, and comparing topic models.
OCTIS estimates optimal hyper-parameters of topic models using Bayesian Optimization.
OCTIS integrates multiple state-of-the-art topic models and evaluation metrics.
OCTIS enables optimization of evaluation metrics as objectives to select hyper-parameter configurations.
OCTIS supports fair comparison of topic models across benchmark datasets using well-known evaluation metrics.
OCTIS allows integration of novel algorithms into the framework.
OCTIS offers interactive visualizations to understand the behavior of each topic model.
A fine-tuning method applies a large number of parallel classifier heads to BERT and enforces orthogonality among the heads.
The method adaptively eliminates weaker classifier heads during training.
The method improves the cross-task generalization ability of fine-tuned BERT models.
The generalization improvement is especially pronounced on relatively smaller-sized datasets.
EMAP generates unsupervised sentence embeddings by projecting sentences onto a fixed-dimensional manifold that preserves local neighborhoods.
EMAP employs a scalable manifold approximation method rooted in topological data analysis for the projection.
EMAP delineates local neighborhoods using set-distance metrics, including Word Mover’s distance.
EMAP attains performance comparable to or better than state-of-the-art methods on multiple publicly available text classification datasets.
European Language Grid (ELG) is a joint platform and marketplace for European language technology stakeholders.
ELG enables stakeholders to upload, share, and distribute language technology services, products, and resources.
ELG will provide access to a large collection of services for all European languages.
ELG will provide access to thousands of datasets for European languages.
ELMoViz adds support for contextualized embedding architectures, specifically for ELMo models, to the WebVectors toolkit.
ELMoViz visualizes input sentences as two-dimensional text by displaying lexical substitutes that are most semantically similar in context.
ELMoViz enables users to select the ELMo layers from which token embeddings are inferred.
ELMoViz provides corpus information about query words and their lexical substitutes, including frequency tiers and parts of speech.
ELMoViz integrates lexical hyperlinks to word representations in static embedding models within the WebVectors toolkit.
Two web services have deployed ELMoViz functionality with pre-trained ELMo models for Russian, Norwegian, and English.
PunKtuator restores punctuation in English, German, French, Hindi, and Tamil using a multitask modeling approach.
PunKtuator does not require extensive knowledge of grammar or syntax of a given language.
PunKtuator works for both spoken and written text.
PunKtuator is the first punctuation restoration system for German and the listed Indic languages and can serve as a baseline for future work.
The concept of nucleus is defined within the Universal Dependencies framework.
Composition functions are introduced to enable a transition-based dependency parser to incorporate nucleus information.
Nucleus composition leads to small but significant improvements in parsing accuracy across twelve languages.
The parsing accuracy improvement primarily affects nominal modifiers, coordination relations, main predicates, and direct objects.
A data-to-text model incorporates three encoders for numerical forecast maps, observation data, and meta-data to generate weather-forecast comments.
Weather labels representing weather conditions such as sunny and rain are introduced to enable explicit description of useful information in generated comments.
Automatic and human evaluations demonstrate that the model achieves higher informativeness than baseline methods.
The correlation between linguistic accommodation and speaker open-mindedness in argumentative discussions is explored.
Analysis of thousands of Reddit Change My View discussions shows that open-mindedness relates to the assumed role of a speaker across contexts.
Discussions that reach agreement exhibit lower levels of linguistic accommodation.
A self-supervised setup uses an individual review as a target summary for a set of similar reviews in unsupervised multi-document opinion summarization.
The setup trains with only a standard log-likelihood loss and mainstream language models.
Control codes are employed to steer generation toward more coherent and relevant summaries, mitigating hallucinations.
A multilingual sentence embedding approach automatically extracts parallel sentences from Wikipedia articles across a wide range of languages, including dialects and low-resource languages.
The extraction process systematically considers all possible language pairs rather than limiting alignments to English.
A freely available corpus of parallel sentences is produced for thousands of language pairs.
Neural machine translation baseline systems trained solely on the mined parallel data achieve strong translation quality on many language pairs when evaluated on the TED corpus.
The mined bitexts enable training machine translation systems between distant languages without requiring a pivot through English.
Dependency parsing is cast as a tree embedding problem.
Geometric properties of dependency trees are incorporated as training losses within a graph-based parser to explicitly preserve structural properties.
The geometric losses yield strong preservation of tree distances.
Parsing performance matches that of a competitive graph-based parser.
An analysis identifies the tree relationships in which parsing errors occur.
A theoretical basis for designing machine reading comprehension datasets grounded in psychology and psychometrics is provided.
Prerequisites for benchmarking machine reading comprehension are summarized.
Future machine reading comprehension datasets should evaluate a model's ability to construct coherent and grounded representations for understanding context-dependent situations.
Future machine reading comprehension datasets should ensure substantive validity by incorporating shortcut-proof questions and explanations as part of the task design.
Monte Carlo dropout provides uncertainty estimates for Transformer-based models.
Uncertainty estimates derived from Monte Carlo dropout improve detection of error-prone instances on natural language understanding tasks.
Computationally inexpensive uncertainty estimates are constructed by combining Monte Carlo dropout with Determinantal Point Processes.
A state-of-the-art semantic parser struggles with disfluent speech in the ATIS dataset.
Adding synthetic disfluencies during training improves semantic parsing performance on disfluent speech.
Synthetic disfluency augmentation can outperform training with real disfluencies for semantic parsing on the ATIS dataset.
A rumor detection method derives a topic mixture vector from an instance's topic distribution and combines it with the instance's vector representation to make predictions.
The rumor detection method outperforms two baseline debiasing methods in cross-topic settings.
When topic-specific words are removed, the rumor detection method performs better than baselines, demonstrating reduced reliance on superficial features.
Synthetic texts contain subtle distinguishing marks inherited from their source language model that can be leveraged for authorship attribution.
Several machine learning-based attribution methods are proposed for attributing synthetic text to its source language model.
A fine-tuned XLNet model can attribute the parent pre-trained language model of synthetic text with high accuracy.
The attribution methods achieve high accuracy across experiments involving synthetic text generated by pre-trained, fine-tuned, or parameter-varied language models.
A new task of event reason extraction from document-level texts using only structural event descriptions is introduced.
FinReason, a large dataset with annotated reason spans for financial events in company announcements, is constructed and released.
Existing canonical event extraction and machine reading comprehension methods are evaluated on the event reason extraction task.
A performance gap between the best existing model and human performance on the task is demonstrated.
The combination of active learning and deep pre-trained models for sequence tagging is investigated thoroughly for the first time.
An extensive empirical study evaluates Bayesian uncertainty estimation methods and Monte Carlo dropout options for deep pre-trained models within an active learning framework for sequence tagging.
The study identifies the best combinations of uncertainty estimation methods and dropout options for different types of deep pre-trained models in active learning for sequence tagging.
A distilled Transformer can replace a full-size Transformer for instance acquisition in active learning, providing improved computational performance.
A computational model of phonetic learning is trained on one or two languages without access to phonology.
The model shows predictable performance on phone-level discrimination tasks.
The model shows predictable performance on word-level discrimination tasks.
The model demonstrates that phonology may not be necessary to explain certain spoken word processing effects observed in non-native speakers.
Analysis of the model’s lexical representation space reveals that the two training languages are not fully separated, resembling the representation of bilingual human speakers.
Adversarial attacks on CNN, LSTM, and Transformer-based text classification models replace words with substitutes that have distinct frequency characteristics.
Frequency-guided word substitutions (FGWS) is an algorithm that detects adversarial examples by exploiting frequency differences between original and substituted words.
FGWS accurately detects adversarial examples on the SST-2 and IMDb sentiment datasets against RoBERTa-based classification models.
FGWS outperforms a recently proposed perturbation discrimination framework in detecting adversarial examples.
Joint energy-based model training during finetuning of pretrained text encoders improves calibration of natural language understanding models while preserving accuracy.
Scalar, hidden, and sharp-hidden energy function variants are defined on top of text encoders for energy-based modeling.
The scalar, hidden, and sharp-hidden energy function variants are experimentally compared for calibration performance.
Noise contrastive estimation is employed to train energy-based models for discrete text data.
An auto-regressive noise model is trained with the masked language model objective to enhance the effectiveness of noise contrastive estimation training.
Sense Clustering over Time (SCoT) is a network-based tool that represents word meanings as clusters of similar words.
SCoT visualizes the formation, change, and disappearance of word senses.

SCoT implements a hybrid approach that aggregates time-stamped documents into intervals, constructs a sense graph for each interval, and merges the static graphs into a dynamic semantic neighbourhood graph over time.
The sense clusters generated by SCoT provide detailed insights into lexical change across continuous intervals while preserving model transparency and provenance.
SCoT has been applied to analyze the evolving meaning of the term "crisis" in a European study.
A dataset of lexical collocation occurrences in context, categorized into 17 representative semantic categories, is constructed.
Unsupervised collocate retrieval using BERT is evaluated for collocation understanding.
Supervised collocation classification in context is evaluated for collocation understanding.
Most language models distinguish light verb constructions well, especially when the collocation’s first argument functions as the subject.
Language models often fail to differentiate different syntactic structures within the same semantic category.
Language models often fail to differentiate fine-grained semantic categories that restrict the use of small sets of valid collocates for a given base.
A systematic evaluation of two conjectures about morphology and error prediction is performed across four tasks and up to 57 languages.
Morphological features improve error prediction in NLP models across multiple tasks and languages.
The predictive power of morphology does not increase with the morphological complexity of a language.
Case and gender are the morphological features most predictive of errors across tasks.
A hierarchical taxonomy for online misogyny is introduced.
An expert-labelled dataset of 6567 Reddit posts and comments for misogyny detection is released.
Robust annotation guidelines for training annotators to identify online misogyny are provided.
Baseline classification performance for binary misogyny detection is reported.
Codebook and datasets are publicly released for future research.
An entropy-regularised reinforcement learning method explores the action space to avoid peaky distributions while maximizing a reward function.
A novel reinforcement learning method employs a dynamic unsupervised reward function to balance exploration and exploitation.
The Soft Actor-Critic framework is adapted as an off-policy maximum entropy model for language generation applications such as machine translation.
Using Soft Actor-Critic with a BLEU reward reduces overfitting to training data and improves performance on out-of-domain machine translation data.
A dynamic unsupervised reward leads to better translation of ambiguous words.
SLTev is an open-source tool that comprehensively evaluates spoken language translation.
SLTev reports quality, latency, and stability of spoken language translation outputs using time‑stamped transcripts and reference translations.
SLTev introduces two new scoring techniques for latency evaluation.
SLTev extends stability measures with a normalized Flicker metric.
SLTev provides a growing collection of test datasets that are directly accessible for comparable system evaluation across papers.
Biomedical entity-aware masking (BEM) encourages masked language models to learn entity-centric knowledge based on pivotal domain entities.
BEM can be applied as a downstream process to a wide variety of masked language models without requiring additional memory or architectural components.
BEM achieves performance comparable to state-of-the-art models on several biomedical question-answering datasets.
D-REPTILE is a meta-learning algorithm designed for few-shot dialogue state tracking.
D-REPTILE improves dialogue state tracking performance in low-data settings across multiple domains, methods, base models, and datasets.
D-REPTILE is model-agnostic and can enhance any existing state-of-the-art dialogue state tracking system on unknown domains.
ℳℱ𝛽 is a decomposable metric for evaluating AMR-to-text generation.
ℳℱ𝛽 measures meaning preservation by reconstructing the input AMR from the generated sentence using state-of-the-art AMR parsers and fine-grained AMR evaluation metrics.
ℳℱ𝛽 measures linguistic form by assessing the generated text with state-of-the-art language models.
Pilot studies demonstrate that satisfying both meaning preservation and form principles improves AMR-to-text evaluation and provides explainable scores.
ℳℱ𝛽 does not require gold AMRs and can be extended to other text generation tasks.
SICK-NL is a Dutch natural language inference dataset created by translating the English SICK dataset.
Baseline evaluation of monolingual and multilingual models is conducted on both SICK and SICK-NL.
Two translation-induced phenomena are encapsulated to construct stress tests for Dutch models.
Stress tests assess Dutch models' handling of syntactic restructurings that preserve semantics.
All evaluated models achieve lower performance on SICK-NL than on SICK, indicating greater difficulty of the Dutch dataset.
Stress test results reveal that Dutch models do not fully capture word order freedom.
T-NER is a Python library for transformer-based named entity recognition finetuning.
T-NER provides a web application that enables interactive model predictions on arbitrary text.
T-NER compiles nine public named entity recognition datasets into a unified format.
T-NER evaluates cross-domain and cross-lingual performance of language models finetuned on named entity recognition across the compiled datasets.
In-domain performance of language models finetuned with T-NER is generally competitive across datasets.
Cross-domain generalization of language models finetuned with T-NER remains challenging even with large pretrained models.
Large pretrained language models can learn domain-specific features when fine-tuned on a combined dataset, improving cross-domain performance.
CAE-T5 is a self-supervised model that suggests civil rephrasings of toxic comments.
CAE-T5 fine-tunes a pre-trained text-to-text transformer using a denoising and cyclic auto-encoder loss.
CAE-T5 generates sentences that are more fluent than those produced by earlier text style transfer systems.
CAE-T5 better preserves the original content of toxic comments compared to earlier text style transfer systems.
A large-scale corpus of claim revision pairs covering diverse topics is compiled.
Two tasks are defined: selecting the better claim in a revision pair and ranking multiple claim versions by quality.
Embedding-based logistic regression and transformer-based neural networks are evaluated for claim quality assessment, showing promising results and cross-topic generalization.
A detailed error analysis identifies quality dimensions of claims that can be reliably assessed.
Data and scripts for reproducing all results are released.
Attentive Relational Network is a metric-based learning architecture for slot tagging.
Attentive Relational Network extends relation networks by incorporating pretrained contextual embeddings such as ELMO and BERT.
Attentive Relational Network incorporates an attention mechanism to improve suitability for natural language processing applications.
Attentive Relational Network achieves superior performance on the SNIPS slot tagging dataset compared to other state-of-the-art metric-based learning methods.
Metric-based learning methods are evaluated for the slot tagging task.
A hybridized method combines an ensemble of simple NMT systems trained with different metrics and an open-source module designed for low-resource machine translation.
The hybridized method achieves higher translation quality than the best individual NMT system.
The hybridized method achieves higher translation quality than the standard ensemble system provided in Marian-NMT.
Improvements of the hybridized method over Marian-NMT are statistically significant in many cases.
Qualitative analysis indicates that the hybridized method is more robust for translating towards morphologically rich languages with limited resources.
A German lexicon with entries ordered by degree of (in)formality is introduced.
Lexicon entries are expanded using sentence-based similarity computations.
Crowdworkers assess the lexical items on a continuous informal‑formal scale to create a gold‑standard evaluation set.
The lexicon enables improved prediction of formality scores by regression models in intrinsic evaluation.
The lexicon is shown to be useful for formality analysis in a German‑language email corpus in extrinsic evaluation.
A model for frame identification generates representations for frames and lexical units from their formal definitions using a pre-trained transformer.
The model assesses the suitability of a frame for a target word based on the semantic coherence of their meanings.
The model achieves consistently better performance than previous systems on three frame identification datasets.
The task of belief-based claim generation is introduced.
Users' beliefs are modeled via their stances on major issues for argumentative text generation.
State-of-the-art text generation models are extended with an additional input that encodes user beliefs.
Automatic evaluation demonstrates that beliefs can be encoded into generated argumentative text.
Manual evaluation reveals that noise from automatically collected bag-of-words reduces effectiveness, and removing this noise mitigates the issue.
RelWalk is a latent variable model for knowledge graph embedding.
The random walk model of word embeddings is extended to knowledge graph embeddings, deriving a scoring function that evaluates the strength of a relation between a head and a tail entity.
Marginal loss minimization corresponds to log-likelihood ratio maximization under probabilities estimated from the knowledge graph embeddings.
A learning objective motivated by the theoretical analysis is proposed for learning knowledge graph embeddings from a given knowledge graph.
The proposed learning objective yields accurate knowledge graph embeddings on the FB15K237 and WN18RR benchmark datasets, providing empirical support for the theory.
Forum 4.0 provides a semi-automatic pipeline to analyze, aggregate, and visualize user comments based on labels defined by domain experts.
Forum 4.0's architecture consists of a containerized system that includes a web-based user interface, a machine learning component, and a task manager for time‑consuming tasks.
Forum 4.0 is applicable to comment‑analytics scenarios in online journalism and app‑store domains.
Machine‑learning experiments with simulated annotations and different sampling strategies on existing datasets evaluate Forum 4.0's performance.
Forum 4.0 attains high classification performance with limited annotated samples by using transformer‑based embeddings together with a lightweight logistic regression model.
Forum 4.0's architecture enables real‑time processing of millions of user comments while maintaining feasible training and classification costs.

Batch size critically influences transformer performance on character-level transduction tasks, and sufficiently large batches enable the transformer to outperform recurrent sequence-to-sequence models.
A simple technique for handling feature-guided character-level transduction is introduced, which further improves performance.
State-of-the-art performance on morphological inflection generation is achieved with the transformer.
State-of-the-art performance on historical text normalization is achieved with the transformer.
The transformer outperforms a strong baseline on grapheme-to-phoneme conversion and transliteration.
ECOL-R is a copy-augmented transformer model for novel object captioning that encourages accurate description of novel object labels.
ECOL-R employs a specialized reward function within the SCST reinforcement learning framework that incentivizes novel object mentions while preserving overall caption quality.
ECOL-R restricts SCST training to images where detected objects appear in reference captions.

ECOL-R improves its copy mechanism by using Abstract Labels to transfer knowledge from known to novel object types and a Morphological Selector to determine appropriate inflected forms of novel object labels.
ECOL-R achieves state-of-the-art performance on the nocaps and held-out COCO benchmarks.
The paraphrase relations required by Allen and Hospedales' explanation of word analogies are not empirically observed in word2vec and GloVe embeddings.
Empirical analysis shows that word2vec and GloVe do not encode linguistic regularities solely when a paraphrase relation holds between the four words of an analogy.
A systematic comparison of twelve transformer-based models for adverse drug event detection is conducted on two standard benchmarks.
SpanBERT and PubMedBERT achieve the best performance among the evaluated models for adverse drug event detection.
Span-based pretraining provides a decisive advantage for precise recognition of adverse drug events.
In-domain language pretraining is particularly beneficial when a transformer model is trained from scratch on biomedical text for adverse drug event detection.
A directed syntactic dependency graph based on a dependency tree is designed to establish paths from opinion targets to candidate opinion words.
An attention-based relational graph convolutional neural network (ARGCN) is introduced to exploit syntactic information over dependency graphs for target-oriented opinion words extraction.
A target-aware representation is incorporated to encode target information for explicit extraction of opinion words toward a given opinion target.
The proposed model outperforms existing models on four benchmark datasets for target-oriented opinion words extraction.
Component-wise analysis demonstrates the effectiveness of each part of the proposed model.
Shallow decoders increase decoder speed in many-to-one multilingual translation without sacrificing translation quality.
Shallow decoders cause a quality drop in one-to-many multilingual translation.
DEMSD employs a deep encoder and multiple shallow decoders, each dedicated to a disjoint subset of target languages.
DEMSD achieves faster inference than standard transformer models without loss in translation quality.
The optimal next step of an arc-eager parser for a non-projective dependency structure can be calculated in cubic time.
Calculating the optimal next step in cubic time solves an open problem in parsing theory.
The cubic-time calculation enables training of parsers using a dynamic oracle.
Us vs. Them dataset comprises 6,861 Reddit comments annotated for populist attitudes.
Large-scale computational models of populist attitudes are introduced.
Baselines are established for two tasks associated with populist attitudes.
Multi-task learning models that use emotion and group identification as auxiliary tasks are presented.
Multi-task models demonstrate that incorporating emotion and group identification improves performance on populist attitude tasks.
An analysis reveals a relationship between populist mindsets, social groups, and a range of associated emotions.
LOME identifies spans of entity and event mentions in multilingual text using a FrameNet parser.
LOME performs coreference resolution, fine-grained entity typing, and temporal relation prediction between events.
LOME constructs an event- and entity-focused knowledge graph from extracted information.
LOME enables integration of third-party modules for additional annotation types such as relation extraction.
LOME achieves performance that outperforms or matches monolingual state-of-the-art methods by leveraging multilingual encoders such as XLM-R and multilingual training data.
Learning-to-Compare (L2C) model learns semantic structures of two images and generates comparative descriptions.
Learning-to-Compare benefits from comparing explicit semantic representations with single-image captions.
Learning-to-Compare generalizes better to new testing image pairs.
Learning-to-Compare achieves superior automatic and human evaluation performance on the Birds-to-Words dataset compared to baseline.
A supervised learning scenario that trains an agent to mimic successful guessing games is introduced.
Self-play via Iterated Experience Learning (SPIEL), a novel method for an agent to play guessing games autonomously, is introduced.
The proposed methods improve in-domain accuracy on the CompGuessWhat?! evaluation suite relative to competitors.
The proposed methods enhance VQA performance on the TDIUC dataset by leveraging more fine-grained object representations learned via SPIEL.
ChainCQG is a two-stage architecture for conversational question generation.
ChainCQG employs a flow propagation training strategy to learn question-answer representations across multiple dialogue turns.
ChainCQG generates synthetic conversations for training and evaluation of conversational question-answering systems.
ChainCQG generates different types of questions with improved fluidity and coreference alignment.
ChainCQG outperforms answer-aware and answer-unaware state-of-the-art baselines on conversational question generation.
The evolution of embedded syntax trees in BERT is analyzed during fine-tuning across six supervised tasks covering all levels of linguistic structure.
Fine-tuning BERT for part-of-speech tagging leads to forgetting of encoded syntactic information.
Fine-tuning BERT for dependency parsing and constituency parsing reinforces encoded syntactic information.
Fine-tuning BERT for semantics-related tasks preserves encoded syntactic information.

A protocol for incrementally recording the minimal edits to source texts in black-box neural machine translation was implemented, enabling the collection of thousands of pre-editing instances across multiple language directions, systems, and domains.
The collected pre-editing instances were analyzed to describe characteristics of pre-edited source texts, the diversity of editing operations, and the impact of those operations on NMT outputs.
Enhancing the explicitness of meaning and syntactic structure in source texts contributes more to translation quality than reducing length or simplifying the text.
While the impact of pre-editing on black-box NMT outputs is generally unpredictable, certain types of editing operations lead to consistent tendencies in the resulting translations.
Style-Transfer Transformer (STT) incorporates a stepped styled adaptive layer-normalization into a transformer network to transfer audio and video style information onto text representations.
STT learns richer representations for multi-modal utterances by applying style transfer of tone of voice and facial expressions to text.
STT achieves performance comparable to state-of-the-art models on sentiment analysis and emotion recognition while using substantially fewer model parameters.
An analysis of the relative contributions of audio and video modalities is performed for downstream sentiment analysis and emotion recognition tasks.
CADLAC is a multi-modal conversational agent system that simulates individuals with varying abilities in activities of daily living for training professional assessors.
CADLAC incorporates a bidirectional LSTM topic tracker that enables navigation of conversations across eighteen ADL domains.
CADLAC includes a dialogue manager that accesses a database of historical ADL assessments to inform interactions.
CADLAC employs a rule-based natural language generation module for producing domain-specific responses.
CADLAC integrates a pre-trained GPT-2 based open-domain sub-agent to handle conversation turns outside the ADL domains.
A method for online news stream clustering extends a non-parametric streaming K-means algorithm.
The method combines sparse and dense document representations and aggregates document‑cluster similarity across these representations.
A neural classifier makes clustering decisions based on the aggregated similarity scores.
Weighted document‑cluster similarity is learned by adapting the triplet loss into a linear classification objective.
Fine‑tuning objectives and external knowledge incorporated into pre‑trained transformer models improve the effectiveness of contextual embeddings for clustering.
The method achieves state‑of‑the‑art performance on a standard English news stream clustering dataset.
Skew and stereotype are introduced as metrics that quantify gender bias in contextual language models on the WinoBias pronoun resolution task.
Gender stereotype correlates approximately negatively with gender skew in out-of-the-box models, indicating a trade-off between these bias forms.
An online mitigation method removes gender skew while increasing gender stereotype.
Fine-tuning BERT with an augmented gender-balanced dataset reduces both gender skew and gender stereotype compared to fine-tuning with an unaugmented dataset.
Existing gender bias benchmarks do not fully probe professional bias because pronoun resolution may be obscured by cross-correlations from other gender prejudice manifestations.
Three normalization models specifically designed for code-switched data are proposed.
Normalization layers and corresponding language ID and POS tags are introduced for a Turkish-German code-switched dataset.
The proposed code-switched normalization models are evaluated on Indonesian-English and Turkish-German language pairs.
The code-switched normalization models achieve higher performance than monolingual normalization models.
Normalization of code-switched data improves downstream POS tagging performance compared to unnormalized input.
PHASE is a time-and phase-aware framework that adaptively learns features from a user’s historical emotional spectrum on Twitter for preliminary screening of suicidal risk.
PHASE learns phase-like progressions in users’ historical Plutchik-wheel-based emotions to contextualize suicidal intent.
PHASE outperforms state-of-the-art methods for suicide ideation detection on social media.
VoiSeR is a large-scale dataset of voice-based search refinements.
VoiSeR was collected using a novel crowdsourcing task.
VoiSeR includes manual annotations of specific refinement intents.
Qualitative and empirical analysis identifies the most common and challenging types of voice-based search refinements for conversational systems.
VoiSeR supports research on conversational query understanding and contextual user intent prediction.
ExBAN corpus provides crowd-sourced natural language explanations for Bayesian Networks.
Correlation analysis compares human subjective ratings of natural language explanations with automatic NLG evaluation measures.

Embedding-based automatic NLG evaluation methods, such as BERTScore and BLEURT, exhibit higher correlation with human ratings of natural language explanations than word-overlap metrics, such as BLEU and ROUGE.
Adversarial learning with multiple diverse discriminators that are encouraged to learn orthogonal hidden representations is introduced.
The method reduces model bias more than standard adversarial removal methods.
The method improves training stability compared to standard adversarial removal methods.
Three types of multi-split based reversible transformers are designed.
A backpropagation algorithm for reversible transformers is devised that avoids storing activations for most layers.
Two fine-tuning techniques, splits shuffle and self ensemble, are introduced to boost translation accuracy of reversible transformers.
Reversible transformer models achieve higher translation quality than vanilla transformers on multiple datasets.

Large-scale reversible transformer models achieve competitive BLEU scores on WMT’14 English‑German and English‑French translation while using less than half of the training memory required by standard transformers.
A heterogeneous graph model for extractive summarization incorporates both discourse relations and coreference relations.
The heterogeneous graph contains three types of nodes, each representing text spans of a different granularity.
The proposed model demonstrates effectiveness on a benchmark summarization dataset.
Choosing models based on task success in end-to-end referential dialogue guessing games prevents them from learning linguistically richer dialogues.
The discrepancy between task-success-driven model selection and language quality is consistent across different models and tasks such as GuessWhat, GuessWhich, and Mutual Friends.
In the GuessWhat game, models can increase accuracy by learning to ground, encode, and decode words that are infrequent in the training set.
A contrastive learning strategy generates questions that are closely related to a positive document set while being distant from a negative document set.
Multi-Source Coordinated Question Generator (MSCQG) includes a supervised learning stage that trains a single-document question generator.
MSCQG includes a reinforcement learning stage that trains a coordinator model to find optimal attention weights aligning multiple single-document generators to promote question specificity.
Set-induced Contrastive Regularization (SCR) is an auxiliary objective that improves the coordinator’s contrastive learning during reinforcement learning.
MSCQG significantly outperforms several strong baselines according to automatic metrics and human evaluation.
Classifiers trained on mBERT embeddings of transitive sentences recover subjecthood in zero-shot evaluation on intransitive sentences across languages.
Classifier decision distributions reflect the morphosyntactic alignment of the languages used for training.
mBERT representations are influenced by high-level grammatical features that are not explicitly present in individual input sentences.
Passive voice, animacy, and case strongly correlate with subjecthood classification decisions made by the trained classifiers.
Subjecthood encoding in mBERT is continuous and depends on semantic and discourse factors rather than being purely syntactic.
SMiLER provides 1.1 million annotated sentences covering 36 relations across 14 languages for joint entity and relation extraction.
SMiLER is the largest and most comprehensive multilingual dataset for joint entity and relation extraction.
HERBERTa combines two independent BERT models, one for sequence classification and one for entity tagging, in a pipeline for joint entity and relation extraction.
HERBERTa achieves near‑state‑of‑the‑art performance on English joint entity and relation extraction, comparable to the current SOTA on CoNLL and SpERT.
A transformer-based extension of a lexical replacement attack for author profiling is introduced.
When trained on a weakly labeled corpus, the transformer-based attack attains high transferability and reduces target model performance below chance.
The attacks are notably less detectable by humans than prior attacks.
A set of components is proposed to enable deployment of adversarial stylometry attacks without access to data or target models.

The impact of subword pooling choices on downstream performance is evaluated on morphological probing, part-of-speech tagging, and named entity recognition across nine typologically diverse languages using mBERT and XLM-RoBERTa.
Attention over subwords yields the best performance on morphological probing tasks compared to the commonly used first-subword strategy.
A small LSTM applied to subwords provides the best performance on part-of-speech tagging.
A small LSTM applied to subwords provides the best performance on named entity recognition.
mBERT outperforms XLM-RoBERTa on part-of-speech tagging and named entity recognition across the nine languages.
LSOIE is a large-scale Open Information Extraction dataset created by converting the QA‑SRL 2.0 dataset.
LSOIE contains roughly twenty times more human‑annotated OIE tuples than the previously largest human‑annotated OIE dataset.
Benchmark OIE models evaluated on LSOIE establish baseline performance for future improvements.
Alternative Answer Sets (AAS) provide multiple ground-truth answers for visual question answering questions.
Alternative Answer Sets are generated automatically with off-the-shelf natural language processing tools.
A semantic metric based on Alternative Answer Sets evaluates VQA performance with multiple plausible answers.
Top VQA solvers are modified to support multiple plausible answers for each question.
Applying Alternative Answer Sets and the semantic metric to the GQA dataset yields performance improvements.
A fine-grained event detection dataset comprising 449 event types is introduced.
The Semcor word sense disambiguation dataset is transformed into a large, high-quality dataset for fine-grained event detection.
Current event detection methods exhibit limited performance on the fine-grained dataset, revealing substantial challenges.

A two-stage cross-lingual transfer method combines annotation projection and model transfer of pre-trained language models to capture both surface-level and semantic-level cross-linguality for semantic dependency parsing.
The graph-based semantic dependency parser using this method achieves performance close to the approximated upper bound.
This work constitutes the first report on cross-lingual transfer for semantic dependency parsing.
Language modelling is framed as a multi-task problem.
Language models exhibit emergent multi-task behavior within the standard language modelling objective.
The generalisation behaviour of language models on Negative Polarity Items is analyzed, revealing alignment with multi-task learning principles.
Syntax-BERT is a plug-and-play framework that incorporates syntax trees into arbitrary pre-trained Transformer checkpoints.
Syntax-BERT enables consistent improvement on natural language understanding tasks across multiple pre-trained models such as BERT, RoBERTa, and T5.
Experiments verify that syntax trees are effective for natural language understanding when integrated via Syntax-BERT.
Representing tweets with contextualized embeddings improves user-level stance detection compared to strong baselines.
Expanding a user's tweets with their timeline tweets and clustering users enables unsupervised stance detection with high accuracy for less vocal users.
User stance detection can be performed effectively using only a few topical tweets per user.
A framework displays multiple candidate upcoming topics and allows a user to select a subset to guide Transformer-based language generation.
A method produces candidate topics by predicting the centers of word clusters in possible continuations.
A text generation model generates output that adheres to the topics chosen by the user.
Training of both the topic prediction method and the controllable generation model is performed in a self-supervised manner using only unlabeled text.
The candidate topic options generated by the method outperform those obtained from standard clustering approaches.
The framework frequently generates fluent sentences that are related to the selected topics, as judged by automated metrics and crowdsourced evaluations.
Dense fine-tuned vector-based linguistic information is extracted from BERT to augment neural machine translation.
Incorporating the extracted linguistic information enables neural machine translation to generalize better across diverse training contexts.
Augmenting neural machine translation with the extracted linguistic information does not increase training difficulty relative to conventional Transformer-based neural machine translation.
An in-depth study of English character embeddings is conducted using grapheme–color synesthesia resources.
Ten different character embeddings are compared with synesthete perceptions of character similarity.
LSTM-based character embeddings align more closely with human synesthetic perception than transformer-based embeddings.
Character embeddings derived from grapheme-to-phoneme conversion are the most similar to human synesthetic perception among the evaluated models.
ELMo character embeddings differ from both human synesthetic perception and other model embeddings.
A novel dataset of implicitly abusive comparisons was created via crowdsourcing with measures to ensure representativeness and unbiasedness.
Classification experiments employing a range of linguistic features were performed to investigate mechanisms underlying implicitly abusive comparisons.
A corpus of American news annotated for civility is introduced.
The Jigsaw Perspective API is evaluated for detecting incivility on the American news corpus.
Toxicity models exemplified by the Perspective API are found to be inadequate for analyzing incivility in news.
Error analysis identifies spurious correlations between frequently mentioned identity descriptors and incivility in toxicity model predictions.
Mini-batch embedding (MBE) represents features of sentences in a mini-batch.
Mini-batch embedding constructs mini-batches by selecting sentences from the same document to capture inter-sentence context.
Mini-batch embedding is incorporated into neural machine translation models.
Incorporating mini-batch embedding into neural machine translation models consistently outperforms strong baseline translation capabilities.
Incorporating mini-batch embedding improves writing style and terminology to fit the document's context.
A summarization framework uses dense vector representations of all input reviews as input to an abstractive model, eliminating reliance on pre-selected content.
The framework includes a zero-shot customization technique that incorporates user preferences into generated summaries.
An instantiated model based on the framework generates more informative opinion summaries than prior methods.
The instantiated model achieves better performance than previous state-of-the-art methods on the Rotten Tomatoes dataset.
A measure of faithfulness for neural machine translation is defined based on stress tests that perturb attention weights crucial for prediction and evaluate resulting changes in output.
A novel differentiable objective that rewards faithful behavior through probability divergence is introduced for neural machine translation models.
The differentiable objective increases the faithfulness of attention explanations in neural machine translation models.
The objective produces more trustworthy attention heatmaps and enables useful analysis of neural machine translation model behavior.
The objective improves faithfulness without reducing translation quality and can act as a regularizer that sometimes improves translation quality.
A multi-task view (MTV) of data augmentation is introduced, where the primary task trains on original examples and an auxiliary task trains on augmented examples.
MTV weights original and augmented samples substantively during training, relaxing the requirement that augmented examples closely resemble original data.
MTV enables the use of stronger augmentation functions.
Empirical experiments on three benchmark text classification datasets with four common augmentation techniques show that MTV yields higher and more robust performance than traditional augmentation.
Mixed-vocabulary training aligns teacher and student embeddings in knowledge distillation for BERT.
The distillation method produces a task-agnostic BERT-LARGE student model with reduced vocabulary and hidden dimensions.
The student model is an order of magnitude smaller than previously distilled BERT models.
The student model achieves a superior size-accuracy trade-off on language understanding benchmarks and a practical dialogue task.
Machine translationese is defined as an artificially impoverished language resulting from algorithmic bias in machine translation.
Lexical and morphological richness of translations from phrase-based statistical machine translation and neural machine translation are empirically evaluated for English-French and English-Spanish.
All investigated machine translation paradigms (phrase-based statistical and neural) exhibit a loss of lexical and syntactic richness in English-French and English-Spanish translations.
Quotability identification is formulated as a passage ranking problem.
Feature-based models are evaluated for ranking passages by predicted quotability.
BERT-based models are evaluated for ranking passages by predicted quotability.
A RoBERTA sequential sentence tagger achieves the highest performance among evaluated models for quotability identification.
Experiments are conducted on five datasets covering English and Latin texts from poetry, plays, and novels, with derived documents such as news and journal articles.
AREDSUM-SEQ jointly considers salience and novelty during sentence selection.
AREDSUM-CTX scores salience first and then learns to balance salience and redundancy, enabling measurement of the impact of each aspect.
AREDSUM-CTX achieves better performance than AREDSUM-SEQ and state-of-the-art extractive summarization baselines.
Adaptive redundancy-aware iterative sentence ranking models are introduced for extractive document summarization.

An approach efficiently incorporates contextual information into Answer Sentence Selection models by extracting relevant sentences with unsupervised similarity techniques and feeding them to a transformer fine-tuned for Answer Sentence Selection.
The approach leverages a multi-way attention architecture to encode context efficiently.
The approach achieves higher accuracy than non-contextual state-of-the-art Answer Sentence Selection models while maintaining minimal impact on system latency.
A joint learning formulation simultaneously learns classifier parameters and hyperbolic label embeddings for hierarchical multi-label classification without requiring a known label hierarchy.
Joint learning improves classification performance compared to baselines that use label co-occurrence based pre-trained hyperbolic embeddings.
The jointly learned classifiers achieve state-of-the-art generalization on standard hierarchical multi-label classification benchmarks.
Hyperbolic label embeddings obtained via joint learning represent the label hierarchy more accurately than alternative embedding methods.
Metric-type identification from multi-level header numerical tables is defined as a new information extraction task.
A dataset of multi-level header numerical tables with headers, captions, and metric-types extracted from scientific papers is provided.
Joint-learning neural classification and generation schemes based on a pointer-generator architecture are proposed for metric-type identification.
Joint-learning neural classification and generation schemes based on BERT are proposed for metric-type identification.
Joint models can handle both in-header and out-of-header metric-type identification problems.
Table representations are refined using surrounding textual context to improve question answering from tables.
Textual and table-based predictions are combined using an effective method for question answering from full documents.
The combined approach yields significant improvements on the Natural Questions dataset.
Process Execution Graphs provide a document-level representation of wet lab biochemistry protocols that captures cross-sentence relations, long-range coreference, grounding, and implicit arguments.
An interactive textual simulator that tracks entity traits and semantic constraints is used to manually annotate Process Execution Graphs in a corpus of complex lab protocols.
Graph-prediction models trained on the annotated Process Execution Graph corpus are effective at entity identification in wet lab protocols.
Graph-prediction models trained on the annotated Process Execution Graph corpus are effective at local relation extraction in wet lab protocols.
The Process Execution Graph corpus facilitates further exploration of challenging long-range relations in wet lab protocols.
LI VERB KAA-OOSHITAHK DI MICHIF is a finite-state computational model of Michif verbal morphology.
LI VERB KAA-OOSHITAHK DI MICHIF uses composed finite-state transducers to model concatenative morphology and phonological rule alternations unique to Michif.
A rule-based finite-state approach is necessary for modeling Michif verbal morphology because of insufficient language data for machine‑learning methods.
LI VERB KAA-OOSHITAHK DI MICHIF supports the creation of tools for documentation, education, and revitalization of the Michif language.
Discrete sarcasm detection features are incorporated into argumentative relation classification.
Multitask learning of argumentative relation classification and sarcasm detection with a dual LSTM and hierarchical attention improves argumentative relation classification.
Multitask learning of argumentative relation classification and sarcasm detection with Transformer-based architectures improves argumentative relation classification.
Modeling sarcasm improves argumentative relation classification across all experimental setups.

A search-based paraphrase generation scheme generates candidate paraphrases via iterated transformations and evaluates them using syntax quality, BERT-derived semantic distance, and GPT2-perplexity-based lexical quality.
Monte-Carlo Tree Search for Paraphrase Generation (MCPG) addresses the multi-objective search problem in paraphrase generation.
Pareto Tree Search (PTS) addresses the multi-objective search problem in paraphrase generation.

Extensive experiments on multiple datasets demonstrate that the proposed algorithms achieve competitive performance compared to state-of-the-art paraphrase generation methods despite being unsupervised.
A three-step approach automatically detects exaggerated statements in health science news.
The approach extracts relation phrases that connect dependent and independent variables in scientific statements.
The approach classifies the strength of extracted relation phrases.
The approach compares the strengths of relation phrases to identify whether one statement exaggerates another and to what extent.
The approach outperforms baseline models that compare state-of-the-art embeddings with a binary classifier or recast the task as textual entailment.
Annealing-KD trains a student model with gradually annealed soft-targets generated by the teacher at different temperatures.
Annealing-KD mitigates the difficulty of knowledge distillation when the teacher-student capacity gap is large.
Empirical experiments show that Annealing-KD achieves superior results on image classification tasks CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100.
Empirical experiments show that Annealing-KD achieves superior results on language inference tasks in the GLUE benchmark with BERT-based models.
The paper provides theoretical analysis supporting the effectiveness of Annealing-KD.
Late fusion applies a graph neural network to transformer outputs to incorporate dependency tree information.
Joint fusion infuses dependency tree structure into transformer attention layers.
Additional model design elements are introduced that enable improved performance of syntax-infused transformers.
Syntax-infused transformers achieve state-of-the-art results on semantic role labeling and relation extraction tasks.
Performance gains of syntax-infused transformers depend heavily on the availability of human-annotated dependency parses.
Existing methods for comparing the informativeness of early versus later word segments contain confounds.
New measures that avoid these confounds are introduced.
Controlling for confounds reveals a cross-linguistic tendency to front-load information in words across hundreds of languages.
ENPAR is a pre-training method that improves joint entity and relation extraction performance.
ENPAR requires only additional entity annotations, which are easier to collect than annotations for tasks such as coreference resolution or event extraction.
ENPAR incorporates both entity information and entity pair information into the encoding process.
ENPAR pre-trains an entity encoder and an entity pair encoder using four objectives: masked entity typing, masked entity prediction, adversarial context discrimination, and permutation prediction.
ENPAR outperforms BERT on the ACE05, SciERC, and NYT datasets and outperforms the previous state-of-the-art on ACE05.
Incremental beam manipulation reranks hypotheses during decoding instead of only at the end.
Incremental beam manipulation discards hypotheses unlikely to lead to a good final output and considers previously ignored hypotheses.
Incremental beam manipulation improves BLEU scores compared to vanilla beam search on the E2E and WebNLG challenges.
Incremental beam manipulation outperforms a strong reranker on the E2E challenge and achieves comparable performance on the WebNLG dataset.
A graph-based fake news detection method uses a summarization technique that relies solely on internal document information.
The method represents relationships among all sentences with a graph.
The method computes a reflection rate of contextual information among sentences via an attention mechanism.
The method leverages summary information as an important subject of the document to improve detection performance.
The method achieves higher accuracy than previous fake news detection approaches.
A method estimates word polysemy using simple geometry in contextual embedding space.
The method operates fully unsupervised and purely data-driven.
The method's polysemy rankings are strongly correlated with multiple human-constructed resources across standard metrics, with statistical significance.
The method enables sampling of sentences containing different senses of a given word without additional cost.
The method is applicable to any language.
A multilingual probe predicts tense from BERT sentence representations for French and Chinese.
French tense can be accurately predicted from BERT sentence representations using surface morphological cues.
Chinese tense prediction from BERT sentence representations performs poorly, indicating limited capture of abstract linguistic cues.
The performance disparity between French and Chinese suggests that BERT tends to memorize shallow patterns rather than learn abstract linguistic properties.
A systematic review provides a near-complete snapshot of current reproducibility research in natural language processing.
The review finds that the NLP community lacks consensus on how reproducibility should be defined, measured, and addressed, and that diversity of views is increasing.
Word Sense Disambiguation is reformulated as a multi-label classification task where multiple senses may be assigned to a target word.
The multi-label WSD method can be extended to incorporate structured knowledge from semantic networks in a model-agnostic manner.
The proposed multi-label approach attains state-of-the-art results on English all-words Word Sense Disambiguation.
ENCONTER is an insertion-based transformer that supports hard lexical constraints while maintaining generation efficiency.
ENCONTER introduces a training strategy that incorporates predefined hard lexical constraints such as required entities.
The paper analyzes early termination problems in progressive insertion transformers.
ENCONTER outperforms baseline models on multiple performance metrics, making it more suitable for practical applications.
A linguistically-motivated variant of the encoder-decoder model with attention adds a self-attention module over input substrings to complement character-level cross-attention.
A novel pattern extraction method interprets model behavior by analyzing attention weights.
Pattern extraction applied to cross-attention weights uncovers variation in the form of inflection morphemes.
Pattern extraction from self-attention reveals triggers for variation in inflection morphemes.
Patterns extracted from both cross-attention and self-attention align closely with grammatical inflection classes and class assignment criteria across three typologically diverse languages.
The encoder attention component yields consistent performance improvements over a strong baseline.
The framework expands, retrieves, and infills using flexible templates with a neural sequence-to-sequence model to generate diverse question expressions guided by sentence structure.
An activation/deactivation mechanism for template-based sequence-to-sequence generation learns to discriminate template patterns from content patterns, improving generation quality.

Experiments on two large-scale cross-domain datasets demonstrate that the framework yields more diverse questions while preserving high quality and consistency according to automatic and human evaluations.
Structural encoding of tables improves a language model pre-trained on unstructured text for table-based fact verification.
Pre-training language models on English Wikipedia table data improves performance on table-based fact verification.
Pre-training on a question answering task with column-level cell rank information yields the best performance for table-based fact verification.
Improved pre-training and cell embeddings outperform the state-of-the-art Numerically-aware Graph Neural Network table fact verification model.
Incorporating numerical information with cell rankings and question-answering pre-training further increases accuracy for table-based fact verification.
A method automatically rewrites queries into "BERTese", a paraphrase query optimized for better knowledge extraction.
Auxiliary loss functions are added to encourage rewritten queries to correspond to actual language tokens.
The method outperforms competing baselines in knowledge extraction, eliminating the need for complex pipelines.
"BERTese" provides insight into the type of language that facilitates knowledge extraction by language models.
BPE and information-theoretic measures are applied to investigate whether orthographic word type distributions become similar at specific subword tokenization levels.
A cross-linguistic comparison involving incremental BPE merges from characters to words is conducted for 47 diverse languages.
Text entropy values converge at particular subword granularity, resulting in the most similar distributions across languages.
The interaction between subword and word-level distributions is analyzed and interpreted in relation to morphological complexity.
A balanced paradigm for automatic WER estimation in a classification setting is introduced.
WER-BERT, a BERT-based architecture incorporating speech features, is proposed for automatic WER estimation.
A distance loss function is introduced to address the ordinal nature of e-WER classification.
The proposed approach achieves state-of-the-art performance in automatic WER estimation on the Librispeech dataset and a commercial ASR system.
An approach enhances aspect-level sentiment analysis by modeling dependency type information with key-value memory networks.
The approach selectively leverages dependency results to mitigate noise from parsing errors.
The approach outperforms baseline models on five benchmark datasets for aspect-level sentiment analysis.
The approach achieves state-of-the-art performance on three benchmark datasets for aspect-level sentiment analysis.
Performance predictors are evaluated on fine-grained performance measures such as per-class accuracy.
Methods for assessing the reliability of performance prediction models using confidence intervals and calibration are proposed.
An analysis across four NLP task types demonstrates that fine-grained performance prediction is feasible.
The analysis indicates that reliability analysis is necessary for performance prediction methods.
Recipes for building open-domain chatbots are introduced.
Large-scale language models can learn engaging talking points, knowledge, empathy, personality, and maintain a consistent persona when trained with appropriate data and generation strategy.
Model variants with 90M, 2.7B, and 9.4B parameters are built following the presented recipes.
The built models and code are released publicly.
Human evaluations indicate that the best models surpass existing approaches on engagingness and humanness in multi-turn dialogue.
Failure cases of the models are analyzed to discuss limitations.

Probing measures are introduced to assess whether linguistic properties of noun compounds, especially idiomatic meanings, context dependence, and lexical choice sensitivity, are encoded in standard contextualised word representations.
Noun Compound Senses Dataset is created, comprising noun compounds and their paraphrases in context-neutral and context-informative naturalistic sentences for English and Portuguese.
Empirical evaluation with four probing measures on models such as ELMo and BERT variants shows that contextualised models do not accurately represent idiomaticity.
Statistical entropy measures are used to provide an updated complexity analysis of the Most Probable Sentence problem for probabilistic context-free grammars.
Upper bounds on running time and error are derived for a simple search algorithm solving the Most Probable Sentence problem.
The derived bounds enable a search algorithm that runs faster than implied by the NP-completeness of the Most Probable Sentence problem.
The complexity analysis and algorithmic bounds are applied to word sense disambiguation and inference tasks.
An analysis of propaganda on six major political Reddit forums in the US and UK is presented.
The analysis identifies which users post propaganda on those forums.
The analysis compares how propaganda differs across the political spectrum on those forums.
The analysis examines how propaganda is received on those forums.
Selection bias during data collection introduces artifacts in unreliable news detection datasets.
Overlapping article sources between training and evaluation data enable models to memorize site-label mappings rather than learning the unreliable news detection task.
Model accuracy on unreliable news detection decreases markedly when evaluated on a split without train/test source overlap.
Practical guidelines for constructing more reliable unreliable news detection datasets are presented.
A simple model can be used as a difficulty or bias probe, and clean non-overlapping site and date splits can be employed to reduce confounding in unreliable news detection experiments.
Cognitive features extracted from human readers' gaze behavior are incorporated to enrich feature sets for cognate detection.
Enriched feature sets with extracted cognitive features improve cognate detection performance compared to prior orthographic, phonetic, and semantic similarity based approaches.
Predicted cognitive features derived from gaze data for a larger sample also improve cognate detection performance.
The collected gaze behavior dataset for cognates is released publicly.
Cross-lingual cognate detection models trained with cognitive features are released publicly.
RecHyperNet is a recursive hypergraph network for knowledge base question answering.
RecHyperNet models knowledge bases using recursive hypergraphs to organize group relationships.
RecHyperNet demonstrates effectiveness on multiple knowledge base question answering benchmarks.
WDLMPro evaluates word understanding of language models using dictionary definitions of words.
Three popular pretrained language models struggle to match words with their definitions in WDLMPro.
The difficulty of WDLMPro reveals that current language models have limited word understanding.
A method projects English AMR annotations to other languages using weak supervision.
A novel technique aligns foreign-language tokens to English AMR nodes via contextual word alignment.
The foreign-to-English AMR alignment technique is weakly supervised and relies on contextualized XLM‑R word embeddings.
Multilingual AMR systems built with this approach achieve performance that surpasses the best published results for German, Italian, Spanish, and Chinese.
Language Transmission Simulator models both cultural and architectural evolution in a population of agents.
Incorporating learning biases of language learners and allowing language and agents to co-evolve yields optimal outcomes.
Architectural evolution of the agent population improves all considered metrics beyond the gains achieved by cultural transmission alone.
Investigating underlying agent architecture is important for language emergence research.
A representation learning framework integrates social commonsense knowledge with lifelong language learning for social event representation.
Methods incorporate pragmatic aspects into social event embeddings by leveraging social commonsense knowledge.
Continual learning strategies enable incremental consolidation of new knowledge while retaining prior knowledge in social event embeddings.
Social event embeddings demonstrate improved performance on event similarity, reasoning, and paraphrase detection tasks.

Traditional and neural coherence models are benchmarked on synthetic sentence ordering tasks and on downstream applications including machine translation, summarization, and retrieval-based dialog next utterance prediction.
Model performance on synthetic sentence ordering tasks exhibits weak correlation with performance on downstream coherence evaluation tasks.
Regulatory information retrieval (REG-IR) applies document-to-document information retrieval with whole documents as queries.
Two datasets linking EU directives and UK legislation are compiled and released.
Fine-tuning a BERT model on an in-domain classification task yields the best document representations for information retrieval.
Neural re-rankers underperform because contradictory supervision creates bias toward the pre-fetcher’s scores.
Applying a date filter improves retrieval performance, highlighting the importance of temporal information.
A system automatically expands verbatim transcripts of video-recorded conversations with multimodal data streams.
The system weaves multimodal annotations into verbatim transcripts using preprocessing rules to promote interpretability.
The range of multimodal features relevant for detecting rapport-building is identified.
Expanding multimodal annotations leads to statistically significant improvements in detecting rapport-building.
Full dependency trees can be decoded from single attention heads of multilingual BERT at accuracy above baseline.
Individual dependency relations are often tracked by the same attention heads across multiple languages in multilingual BERT.
Fine-tuning multilingual BERT on a supervised parsing objective while freezing specific parameter subsets yields attention patterns that still encode linguistic structure.
The encoded linguistic structure in attention patterns varies depending on which parameters are frozen during fine-tuning.
Attention patterns of multilingual BERT reflect dependency syntax across eighteen languages.
ParaSCI provides a large-scale scientific paraphrase dataset with longer and more diverse paraphrases than existing datasets.
ParaSCI is constructed using intra-paper and inter-paper methods, such as collecting citations to the same paper and aggregating definitions by scientific terms.
PDBERT is a general paraphrase discovery method that leverages partially paraphrased sentences.
ParaSCI achieves satisfactory performance on human evaluation and improves downstream tasks, particularly long paraphrase generation.
Images improve the performance of text classification models that are trained without external pre-training.
Images do not improve the performance of text classification models that incorporate BERT pre-trained on large-scale external data.
A hybrid lemmatization approach combines a sequence-to-sequence neural model with lemmas extracted from an external lexicon or rule‑based system.
The hybrid lemmatizer is trained to both generate lemmas with a sequential decoder and copy lemma characters from external candidates supplied at run‑time.

Integrating candidates from the Apertium morphological analyzer into the hybrid lemmatizer yields statistically significant improvements over baseline models that do not use additional lemma information.
The hybrid lemmatizer outperforms a simple lexicon‑extension method based on the Stanza system.
The hybrid lemmatizer provides complementary improvements relative to a data‑augmentation method for lemmatization.
FakeFlow models the flow of affective information in fake news articles using a neural architecture.
FakeFlow combines topic and affective information extracted from text to learn affective information flow.
Experiments on four real-world datasets demonstrate that FakeFlow outperforms state-of-the-art methods for fake news detection.
