Explanations generated by StyLEx are judged more understandable by humans than saliency-based explanation baselines.
Name-nationality bias in pre-trained language models propagates to downstream summarization tasks as hallucinated factual errors.
More abstractive summarization models allow name-nationality bias to propagate more directly as hallucinated facts.
Altering the adaptation method and fine-tuning dataset reduces the overall rate of hallucinations in summarization.
Changing the adaptation method and fine-tuning dataset does not change the types of name-nationality biases that appear in summarization outputs.
Beam search with large beam sizes produces more faithful abstractive summaries than nucleus sampling.
Ranking beam search candidates using automatic faithfulness metrics improves summary faithfulness.
Incorporating lookahead heuristics that assign a faithfulness score to future summary tokens improves summary faithfulness.
A simple distillation approach enables generation of faithful summaries using greedy decoding.
Faithfulness-aware generation methods improve faithfulness across two datasets as evaluated by multiple automatic metrics and human evaluation.
TrialsSummarizer automatically generates summaries of evidence from randomized controlled trials relevant to a query.
TrialsSummarizer retrieves trial publications matching a query specifying condition, intervention, and outcome, and ranks them by sample size and estimated study quality.
TrialsSummarizer employs a neural multi-document summarization system to produce a synopsis of the top-ranked trials.
A standard sequence-to-sequence model based on BART is evaluated as one architecture within TrialsSummarizer.
A multi-headed architecture is evaluated as an alternative architecture within TrialsSummarizer to provide greater transparency and controllability to end users.
The multi-headed architecture enables users to trace generated tokens back to input sources.
Both evaluated architectures produce fluent and relevant summaries of retrieved evidence.
Both evaluated architectures tend to introduce unsupported statements, making them currently unsuitable for clinical domain use.
A neural module network (SynNaMoN) is constructed from a sentence's syntax parse and trained end-to-end to approximate the sentence embedding generated by a transformer model.
Distillability of a transformer to SynNaMoN captures whether syntax is a strong causal model of the transformer's compositional ability.
Individual SynNaMoN modules are designed with specified internal architecture and linearity to study the geometry of semantic composition.
Distillability varies across sentence embedding models and broadly correlates with their performance, while being relatively insensitive to model size.
Preliminary evidence indicates that syntax-guided composition in sentence embedding models is largely linear, with non-linearities primarily handling non-compositional phrases.
Object hallucination in vision-language pre-trained models is systematically analyzed across model performance, image encoding types, and training objectives.
Higher scores on standard metrics such as CIDEr can correspond to increased object hallucination in vision-language models.
Patch-based image encodings achieve the lowest object hallucination, and decreasing patch resolution further reduces hallucination.
Token-level image-text alignment and controlled generation are identified as crucial factors for reducing object hallucination in vision-language pre-training.
ObjMLM, a novel vision-language pre-training loss, mitigates object hallucination.
ObjMLM reduces object hallucination on both in-domain (COCO Caption) and out-of-domain (NoCaps) evaluation benchmarks.

Slingshot Learning is a semi-supervised method that iteratively and selectively augments a small human-labeled dataset with labels from a high-quality teacher model to improve a student model's performance cost‑efficiently.
Slingshot Learning reduces the performance gap between teacher and student models.
A simple Naive Bayes binary classifier gains performance improvements when taught by ALBERT using Slingshot Learning.
Slingshot Learning is evaluated on several benchmark tasks, demonstrating its effectiveness.
Different variants of the Slingshot Learning algorithm are compared, analyzing performance across multiple scenarios.
A closed-book question generation model employs contrastive learning to capture semantics of long-form abstractive answers.
The model includes an answer reconstruction module to store additional information in its parameters.
The model outperforms baseline methods on public question generation datasets and on the WikiCQA dataset in automatic evaluation.
The model outperforms baseline methods in human evaluation of generated questions.
The model can be used to improve existing closed-book question-answering systems.
Self-adaptive NER retrieves external knowledge from unstructured text to improve recognition of entities that are not well learned.
A two-stage model retrieves unstructured knowledge using uncertain entity predictions as queries.
The model revises entity predictions by concatenating retrieved text to the original input.
The self-adaptive NER model outperforms strong baselines on CrossNER datasets.
A framework dynamically creates temporal test sets of arbitrary time granularity from Wikidata factual data.
The framework constructs fine-grained test splits that separate updated, new, and unchanged facts.
The framework evaluates masked language models using single-token probing, multi-token generation, and MLM scoring.
Eleven pretrained masked language models are evaluated on temporal concept drift using tests derived from Wikidata.
The framework provides a signal indicating when a masked language model has become outdated by leveraging multiple evaluation views.
Self-training reduces flicker in retranslational simultaneous translation.
Self-training improves the flicker‑latency tradeoff while maintaining translation quality comparable to the original approach.
Self-training reduces flicker by controlling monotonicity.
Combining self-training with biased beam search further improves the flicker‑latency tradeoff.
Concept-based Persona eXpansion (CPX) expands short personas into conceptually rich personas for persona‑grounded dialogue models.
CPX comprises a Concept Extractor module and a Sentence Generator module.
CPX is trained using a commonsense dataset that provides concept sets paired with sentences containing those concepts, exploiting task duality.
CPX improves the diversity and richness of responses generated by persona‑grounded dialogue models.
A method compares intrinsic gender bias evaluation measures without using human-annotated examples.
Bias-controlled versions of pre-trained language models are created by fine-tuning with automatically mined male versus female gendered sentences from an unannotated corpus.
Bias-controlled pre-trained language models are evaluated with intrinsic bias evaluation measures and rank correlation is computed between bias scores and the gender proportions used in fine-tuning.
Correlation results from the procedure that does not require human-annotated examples are comparable to those obtained using human-annotated examples.
A novel, flexible, training-efficient architecture combines expert agents by considering questions, answer predictions, and confidence scores to select the best answer among multiple predictions.
The architecture creates collaboration between agents that outperforms previous multi-agent and multi-dataset approaches.
The architecture is highly data-efficient to train.
The architecture can be adapted to any question answering format.
A dataset of answer predictions from expert agents for 16 question answering datasets is released.
A state-of-the-art English-Vietnamese translation model is used to translate biomedical data, producing pretrained and supervised datasets for Vietnamese.
ViPubmedT5 is a pretrained Encoder-Decoder Transformer model trained on 20 million translated PubMed abstracts.
ViPubmedT5 achieves state-of-the-art performance on biomedical summarization and acronym disambiguation benchmarks.
ViMedNLI is a new Vietnamese NLP task created by translating MedNLI and refining it with human experts.
Evaluations of existing methods on ViMedNLI provide comparative performance results with ViPubmedT5.
KGVL-BART generates radiology reports from a frontal and lateral chest X-ray image pair and diagnostic keyword tags.
ChestX-KG, a chest X-ray knowledge graph, is constructed from report text by extracting entity-relation-entity triples with a rule-based tool and verified by experienced radiologists.
KGVL-BART outperforms state-of-the-art transformer-based models on standard natural language generation evaluation metrics for radiology report generation.
Qualitative evaluation by an experienced radiologist indicates that most generated reports are fully correct, with a small proportion completely wrong and some missing important details.
KGVL-BART is the first system to combine multi-modality (dual-view images) and domain knowledge (knowledge graph) for automatic chest X-ray report generation.
Semantic specialization adapts contextualized embeddings to knowledge-based word sense disambiguation using only lexical knowledge.
The method jointly optimizes an Attract-Repel objective for sense pairs and a self‑training objective for context‑sense pairs while constraining deviation from the original embeddings.
When combined with a reranking heuristic that uses the sense inventory, the method achieves state‑of‑the‑art performance on knowledge‑based word sense disambiguation.
Specialized embeddings exhibit similarity patterns that conform to the intended attraction of related senses and repulsion of unrelated senses.
The (dis)similarity of embeddings between related, different, and unrelated senses correlates strongly with word sense disambiguation performance.
A multimodal event transformer provides an event-based reasoning framework for image-guided story ending generation.
Visual and semantic event graphs are constructed from story plots and ending images.
Event-based reasoning is used to mine implicit information within each modality.
Visual and semantic event graphs are connected via cross-modal fusion to integrate multimodal features.
A multimodal injector adaptively passes essential information to the decoder.
An incoherence detection component enhances context understanding of story plots and robustness of graph modeling.
The proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance on image-guided story ending generation.
Tetris defines a new task of Goal-Oriented Script Completion that incorporates a goal together with user preferences and history.
Concept prompting incorporates task-specific concept knowledge into script learning.
Script-oriented contrastive learning reduces step repetition and hallucination in generated scripts.
Concept prompting improves performance on a WikiHow-based script completion dataset.
Script-oriented contrastive learning improves performance on a WikiHow-based script completion dataset.
BERT-family models fine-tuned on question answering are evaluated on comprehension questions derived from garden path and control sentences.
A probing task is used to assess semantic role assignments of BERT-family models for arguments of verbs in garden path and control sentences.
BERT-family models exhibit relatively low performance on question answering for garden path contexts compared to control sentences.
BERT-family models incorrectly assign semantic roles in garden path sentences, with error patterns largely matching human performance.
Transformer language models display garden path effects comparable to human parsing difficulties.
Factual changes between paired documents are represented as question-answer pairs whose answers differ across versions.
DiffQG provides 759 question-answer pairs and 1153 paired passages without factual change.
Question-answer pairs flexibly and concisely capture updated contents of documents.
DiffQG enables evaluation of automatic update summarization as a novel task.
The questions in DiffQG are unambiguous, information-seeking, and involve complex edits that exceed the capabilities of existing question generation and factual change detection systems.
IRMA is a corpus of over 600,000 Italian news articles collected from 56 websites classified as untrustworthy by professional fact-checkers.
IRMA contains more than 335 million tokens.
IRMA provides texts, titles, and dates ranging from 2004 to 2022, along with three semantic measures: keywords, topics at three different resolutions, and LIWC lexical features.
IRMA includes domain-specific metadata such as source type, credibility, and website incoming traffic metrics.
IRMA is the largest Italian misinformation corpus and is freely available as a turnkey resource for developing automatic detection algorithms.
Several controlled sentence sets with predefined lexical and syntactic structures are constructed.
State-of-the-art sentence transformers exhibit a strong nominal-participant-set bias, where cosine similarity between sentence pairs is primarily determined by overlap in noun participants.
Overlap in predicates, lengthy nominal modifiers, or adjuncts has a weaker influence on cosine similarity in sentence transformers compared to noun participant overlap.
Precise syntactic-thematic functions of noun participants have little effect on cosine similarity in sentence transformers.
A probe is designed to investigate the effects of major intrinsic gender bias mitigation strategies on downstream text classification tasks.
Intrinsic mitigation techniques and metrics can hide gender bias while retaining significant gender information in embeddings.
Each mitigation technique can hide bias from some intrinsic bias measures but not all, and each intrinsic bias measure can be fooled by some mitigation techniques but not all.
Intrinsic mitigation techniques alone do not consistently impact extrinsic bias in downstream text classification.
Combining intrinsic bias mitigation techniques with other fairness interventions is recommended for downstream tasks.
Robustness of models trained on generated data is evaluated across multiple augmentation setups on four English offensive language datasets.
The HateCheck functional test suite is used to assess models trained with generative data augmentation for offensive language detection.
Lexical bias issues in generated data for offensive language detection are analyzed qualitatively.
Generative data augmentation yields unreliable improvements in offensive language detection model performance.
Generative data augmentation can have unpredictable effects on lexical bias in offensive language detection models.
LoFT utilizes logic forms as fact verifiers and content planners to control logical table-to-text generation.
LoFT simultaneously addresses unfaithfulness and lack of diversity in logical table-to-text generation.
LoFT demonstrates enhanced faithfulness and diversity of generated sentences on the LogicNLG dataset.
A model that generates questions from relation descriptions enables zero-shot relation extraction without gold question templates.
Generated questions are leveraged to extract the correct tail entity in zero-shot relation extraction.
The method achieves state-of-the-art performance on tail entity extraction in zero-shot relation extraction.
The method approaches the performance of systems that use gold question templates on the RE-QA dataset.
The method outperforms prior zero-shot relation extraction baselines on FewRel and WikiZSL, demonstrating that QA models can match specialized ZRE models without template questions.
Gold-standard word alignments are provided for Bribri–Spanish, Guarani–Spanish, Quechua–Spanish, and Shipibo‑Konibo–Spanish language pairs.
State-of-the-art word alignment methods are evaluated on unseen low-resource languages with and without model adaptation to the target language.
The evaluated word alignments are extrinsically assessed on downstream named entity recognition and part‑of‑speech tagging tasks.
Transformer-based word alignment methods generally outperform traditional alignment models, while both approaches remain competitive.
A set of methods for segmenting longitudinal user posts into timelines that likely contain interesting moments of change in user behavior is proposed.
A novel framework for evaluating timelines is proposed.
The timeline evaluation framework is applied to two different social media datasets.
An analysis of the linguistic content of highly ranked timelines is presented.
Counterfactual analysis attenuates event trigger bias and frequent label bias in temporal relation extraction models.
Incorporating tense information into event representations emphasizes contextual description for temporal relation extraction.
Parameterizing a Dirichlet prior over model-predicted categorical distributions provides uncertainty estimates for temporal relation predictions.
Temperature scaling recalibrates model confidence after bias mitigation in temporal relation extraction.
The model extracts temporal relations and timelines more faithfully than state-of-the-art methods, particularly under distribution shifts.
A masked contrastive loss learns similarities between event mentions by enforcing an attention mechanism over the data minibatch for semi-supervised new event type induction.
Disentangling discovered clusters by approximating the underlying manifolds in the data improves clustering quality as measured by adjusted rand index.
The clustering approach is extended to predict type names for the discovered event clusters.
The discovered event clusters are linked to FrameNet frames.
The method enables semi-supervised induction of new event types without requiring a fully annotated event ontology.
Image captions from the Web are leveraged as a previously underutilized resource for paraphrases.
A mining technology for extracting paraphrases from image captions is introduced.
The Wikipedia-IPC dataset, comprising paraphrase pairs derived from image captions, is released.
Known paraphrase corpora are compared to the Wikipedia-IPC dataset with respect to syntactic and semantic paraphrase similarity.
Characteristic maps along syntactic and semantic similarity dimensions are introduced to identify the style of paraphrases from different sources.
An annotation study demonstrates high reliability of the algorithmically determined characteristic maps.
A novel metric automatically measures the frequency of literal translation errors of idioms in neural machine translation without human involvement.
Controlled translation experiments evaluate models trained with and without test-set idioms across a wide range of global and targeted metrics and test sets.
Monolingual pretraining yields substantial targeted improvements in idiom translation even without observing any translation examples of the test-set idioms.
Randomly initialized models are more local or “myopic,” showing relative insensitivity to variations in idiom context, unlike pretrained models.
Document Flattening (DocFlat) integrates Flat-Batch Attention and Neural Context Gate into a Transformer model to utilize information beyond pseudo-document boundaries.
Flat-Batch Attention enables a model to attend to all positions in a batch and explicitly model relationships between positions.
Neural Context Gate identifies useful information from distant context.
Two variants of DocFlat outperform strong baselines on English-German translation benchmarks with statistical significance.
DocFlat is highly effective at capturing long-range information in document-level neural machine translation.
FeasibilityQA is a question-answering dataset that includes binary classification and multi-choice multi-correct questions to assess understanding of action feasibility.
State-of-the-art language models such as GPT-3, GPT-2, and T5 perform poorly on feasibility questions.
Providing relevant knowledge statements to models yields a modest performance gain on feasibility questions, but overall performance remains low.
A hybrid detection and generation framework for event extraction uses independent encoders for event detection and event argument extraction.
The framework constructs the input for event argument extraction from the output of event detection.
Token-level features are employed to control the fusion of the two encoders, enabling joint bridging training instead of direct representation reuse.
Joint bridging training mitigates feature interference between event detection and argument extraction tasks.
The framework attains competitive results on the ACE05-E, ACE05-E+, and ERE-EN benchmarks, establishing a solid baseline.
HEAR uses a hybrid unidirectional-bidirectional encoder architecture for sequence tagging.
HEAR includes an Adaptive Restart Module that selectively restarts the bidirectional portion of the encoder.
HEAR maintains the performance of bidirectional encoders on offline inputs while improving performance on streaming inputs.
HEAR reduces computational FLOPs in streaming settings compared to naive bidirectional encoders.
HEAR achieves higher streaming exact match accuracy than bidirectional encoders.
A probing framework for power connotation is designed based on an operationalization of connotation frames.
Zero-shot prompting of pre-trained language models predicts power connotation above chance.
Fine-tuning pre-trained language models with the probing framework substantially improves accuracy of power connotation prediction.
Fine-tuned models are applied in a case study of US news reporting on immigration to reveal power dynamics, demonstrating the framework's utility for detecting subtle media bias.
The survey provides the first overview of class imbalance in deep-learning based natural language processing.
The survey categorizes class-imbalance methods for NLP into sampling, data augmentation, loss function choice, staged learning, and model design.
The survey discusses various types of controlled and real-world class imbalance in NLP.
The survey identifies open problems and proposes directions for future research on class imbalance in NLP.
Transformer models are evaluated on natural language model-checking tasks to assess comprehension of logical semantics.
The language fragment used in natural language model-checking tasks significantly impacts transformer performance.
Transformer models can partially understand logical semantics in natural language but cannot fully learn the rules governing the model-checking algorithm.
ViHOS is a human-annotated Vietnamese hate and offensive spans dataset containing 26k spans across 11k comments.
ViHOS provides definitions of hateful and offensive spans in Vietnamese comments together with detailed annotation guidelines.
XLM-R Large achieves the highest performance for single-span detection and all-spans detection on ViHOS.
PhoBERT Large achieves the highest performance for multiple-span detection on ViHOS.
Error analysis demonstrates difficulties in detecting specific types of spans in ViHOS, highlighting challenges for future research.
A weakly supervised dataset generated from traditional classification data can be used to train a textual entailment model for zero-shot text classification.
Empirical study reveals that performance on the entailment proxy task does not exhibit a linear correlation with performance on the target zero-shot text classification task.
Extending fine-tuning beyond the point where the model is still learning can harm zero-shot classification performance.
A straightforward early-stopping method is introduced to halt training at an appropriate time.
SCIROShot, a domain-specific zero-shot text classifier trained on Microsoft Academic Graph data, attains state-of-the-art performance in the scientific domain.
SCIROShot attains competitive performance in domains beyond the scientific domain.
The SCIROShot model and its evaluation benchmark are publicly released.

A multi-agent image reference game is formulated where a speaker is rewarded for describing a target image such that one pretrained listener can correctly identify it among distractors while another listener cannot.

An attention-based adapter between a CLIP vision encoder and a large language model can be finetuned in a contrastive multi-agent setting to induce context-dependent natural language specialization from reward signals alone.
Training a speaker model with two listeners that have different perceptual abilities using this method enables the speaker to adapt to the idiosyncrasies of each listener.
The specialization acquired by the speaker transfers zero-shot to real-world data.
The method provides a way to specialize grounded language models without requiring direct supervision.
An interactive human-in-the-loop topic modeling system provides a user-friendly interface that records and compares each modeling step.
The system includes a novel topic-word suggestion feature that enables users to give feedback aligned with ground truth.
The system supports learning topics from the entire corpus as well as targeted topic modeling for specific corpus aspects.
User studies show that the system adds value in increasingly realistic topic modeling applications.
A multilingual dataset of 1.8 million Reddit comments across 56 subreddits in English, German, Spanish, and French is released for content moderation research.
Extensive experimental analysis reveals challenges of content moderation when decisions are based on rule violations and when moderation rules differ across communities.
The analysis identifies cross-lingual transfer, learning under label noise, transfer of moderation models, and prediction of violated rules as promising research problems.
Counter-GAP is an annotated dataset of gendered ambiguous pronoun coreference instances organized into quadruples.
A method is introduced to collect diverse, natural, minimally distant text pairs via counterfactual generation for bias evaluation.
A bias measurement metric is proposed that uses the difference between inconsistency across genders and within genders to assess bias at the quadruple level.
Four pre-trained language models exhibit greater inconsistency across gender groups than within each group on Counter-GAP.
A name-based counterfactual data augmentation method mitigates gender bias more effectively than an anonymization-based method.
RVL-CDIP benchmark exhibits substantial label noise, estimated at roughly eight percent overall.
RVL-CDIP benchmark contains many ambiguous or multi-label documents.
RVL-CDIP benchmark’s test and training splits overlap considerably, potentially inflating reported model performance.
RVL-CDIP benchmark includes sensitive personally-identifiable information, such as US Social Security numbers.
Recommendations are provided for the desired characteristics of a new document classification benchmark.
Value Zeroing is a novel context mixing score customized for Transformers.
Value Zeroing provides a deeper understanding of how information is mixed at each encoder layer.
Components other than self-attention weights have considerable impact on information mixing in Transformer encoder layers.

Value Zeroing outperforms other analysis methods in evaluating context mixing, as demonstrated by complementary evaluations using linguistically informed rationales, probing, and faithfulness analysis.

A planning model labels each sentence in a document with one of four simplification operations (copy, rephrase, split, delete) by considering a window of surrounding sentences and a token-level representation of the sentence.
The planning model achieves higher accuracy on the sentence-level simplification planning task than strong baseline methods.
Guiding document-level simplification models with the planning model leads to better simplification performance than using strong baseline guidance.
Hippocampal memory indexing controls sample generation in generative replay using compressed features of previous training samples.
Hippocampal memory indexing enables generation of specific training samples from previous tasks in generative replay.
Hippocampal memory indexing improves the balance and quality of generated replay samples.
Hippocampal memory indexing consistently outperforms existing generative replay methods.
A method incorporates question-answering signals into abstractive summarization by selecting salient noun phrases via automatically generated wh-questions.
The method uses a two-stage summarization model where a classification model marks salient noun phrases and a conditional generator produces the summary.
Models trained with QA-based supervision generate higher-quality summaries than baseline methods of identifying salient spans on benchmark summarization datasets.
The content of generated summaries can be controlled by selecting which noun phrases are marked in the input document.
Augmenting training data to align gold summaries with marked input spans enables models to better exclude unmarked document content.
AbLit is a dataset of English literature books with passage-level alignments between original and abridged versions.
The linguistic relations of the original–abridged passage alignments in AbLit are characterized.
Automated models predict the linguistic relations of original–abridged passage alignments.
Automated models generate abridged versions of new texts.
Abridgement of texts is demonstrated to be a challenging task.
A benchmark of multiple transformer variants on several difficult NLP tasks and datasets is provided.
Experiments are designed to isolate the effect of pretraining and hyperparameter settings to assess capacity for long-range attention.
Various methods are presented to investigate attention behaviors beyond metric scores.
Modified attention in long-range transformers offers advantages for content selection.
Modified attention in long-range transformers offers advantages for query-guided decoding.
Modified attention in long-range transformers exhibits insufficient attention to distant tokens.
Modified attention in long-range transformers exhibits accumulated approximation error.

A formal definition of the category of social influence dialogue systems that influence users' cognitive and emotional responses, leading to changes in thoughts, opinions, and behaviors through natural conversations.
A survey compiles tasks, datasets, and methods for social influence dialogue systems across seven diverse domains.
An analysis identifies commonalities and differences among social influence dialogue systems.
An analysis identifies limitations of current research on social influence dialogue systems.
LoRaLay extends the arXiv and PubMed summarization datasets with visual and layout information.
LoRaLay introduces four novel multilingual summarization datasets with layout information for French, Spanish, Portuguese, and Korean.
Baselines that merge layout-aware and long-range models achieve state-of-the-art performance, demonstrating the benefit of combining both approaches.
DiscoScore is a parametrized discourse evaluation metric that uses BERT to model discourse coherence based on Centering theory.
DiscoScore achieves strong system-level correlation with human ratings of coherence, factual consistency, and other aspects, outperforming BARTScore.
Most BERT-based evaluation metrics correlate worse with human-rated coherence than early discourse metrics introduced a decade ago.
BARTScore performs weakly at system-level evaluation of text generation systems.
An analysis demonstrates the importance of discourse coherence for evaluation metrics and explains why one variant of DiscoScore is superior to another.
Controlled text generation is used to perform high-quality data augmentation for relation extraction.
Modifications to the model architecture enable generation of additional training data by interpolating data points in latent space.
Combining the data augmentations with consistency training yields competitive performance for semi-supervised relation extraction on four benchmark datasets.
Efficient Hybrid Generation (EHG) framework is introduced for aspect-based sentiment analysis.
Efficient Hybrid Transformer generates location and semantic information of ABSA targets in parallel.
Global hybrid loss function combined with bipartite matching enables end-to-end training of the EHG model.
EHG framework achieves superior performance compared to state-of-the-art methods on ABSA tasks.
EHG framework achieves higher inference efficiency than existing NLG-based ABSA methods.
Scenario-based semantic parsing is introduced as a variant that first disambiguates an utterance’s scenario before generating its frame.
Retrieve-and-Fill (RAF) architecture comprises a retrieval module that ranks the best scenario given an utterance and a filling module that imputes spans into the scenario to create the frame.
RAF is modular, differentiable, and interpretable.
RAF leverages extra supervision from scenarios.

RAF achieves strong results across high-resource, low-resource, and multilingual settings, outperforming recent approaches while using base pre-trained encoders, small sequence lengths, and parallel decoding.
NusaX provides a parallel sentiment dataset for ten Indonesian local languages.
NusaX includes machine translation datasets for ten Indonesian local languages.
NusaX includes bilingual lexicons for ten Indonesian local languages.
NusaX is the first parallel resource for these low-resource Indonesian languages.
Extensive analyses describe challenges of creating resources for endangered Indonesian languages.
A method makes explicit causal (if‑then, inferential) relations in social commonsense knowledge to identify cultural biases in data.
Augmenting large‑scale language models with social knowledge improves performance on emotion detection and perceived value detection tasks.
Clustering data with social knowledge identifies influential events in datasets.
Leave‑K‑out experiments demonstrate that the identified influential events affect model behavior.
Analyzing causal commonsense relationships reveals influential cultural bias in each dataset.
Detecting mislabeled examples via influential event identification and removing them improves training and performance.
An annotation scheme for feedback comment corpora uses broader-scope comment topics than existing error-correction typologies.
A neural method normalizes temporal expressions using masked language modeling.
The method operates in a multilingual setting.
The method outperforms prior rule-based systems for temporal expression normalization in many languages, especially low-resource languages.
A new in-context learning paradigm measures large language models' ability to learn novel words during inference.
The paradigm rewrites Winograd-style co-reference resolution problems by substituting the key concept word with a synthetic but plausible word.
The benchmark requires models to use a dictionary definition of the new word provided in the prompt.
Large language models' accuracy drops substantially on the benchmark compared to original Winograd tasks, revealing a limitation in current models.
A BERT-based model fine-tuned to encode pairs of discourse units with a linear layer predicts discourse attachments.
Multi-task learning jointly predicts discourse attachment and relation labels.
Multi-task learning improves relation type prediction without degrading attachment performance.
The model achieves state-of-the-art relation labeling performance.
The model allows longer input sizes than previous discourse parsing models.
The model permits attachments where a node has multiple parents, supporting multiparty conversation structures.
MeeT is a multi-task learning-enabled entity tracking approach that leverages knowledge from general-domain tasks.
MeeT fine-tunes T5 with entity tracking-specialized question answering formats.
MeeT employs a customized decoding strategy that satisfies structural constraints of procedural text entity tracking.
MeeT achieves state-of-the-art performance on two popular entity tracking datasets without requiring task-specific architecture design or pre-training.
Larger masked language models achieve better downstream performance with masking rates substantially higher than the conventional 15% rate.
Extremely high masking rates can retain most fine-tuning performance and linguistic probing accuracy.
Uniform token masking requires higher masking rates than span or PMI masking to achieve comparable performance.
Increasing the masking rate introduces more corruption, making the prediction task harder, while also providing more prediction opportunities that benefit optimization.
A framework describing the dual effects of masking rate is used to re-evaluate BERT’s 80-10-10 corruption strategy.
A content selection strategy for question generation that balances relevance and topic diversity.
The relevance-and-diversity content selection strategy improves question generation performance on educational datasets.
Existing question generation evaluation metrics have limitations when applied to content selection for educational question generation.
Multi-feature embedding (MFeEmb) incorporates lexical, dialogue act, and sentiment features to represent dialogue sequences.
Multi-feature embedding improves the generalizability of conflict prediction models across domain shifts.
Inclusion of dialogue act and sentiment features reduces performance loss caused by natural distribution shifts due to vocabulary changes.
Multi-feature embedding serves as a domain-agnostic representation for meta-pretraining few-shot models on collaborative multiparty dialogues.
Generalizability of multi-feature embedding is quantified using the similarity measure proposed by Bontonou et al. (2021).
COMBO is a complete benchmark for open knowledge graph canonicalization.
COMBO provides gold canonicalization for relation phrases in open knowledge graphs.
COMBO provides gold ontology-level canonicalization for noun phrases in open knowledge graphs.
COMBO includes source sentences from which triples are extracted.
COMBO defines metrics for evaluating each type of canonicalization.
Encoding phrases in a triple with pretrained language models improves relation canonicalization.
Encoding phrases in a triple with pretrained language models improves ontology-level canonicalization of noun phrases.
MEGAnno+ is a human-LLM collaborative annotation system.
MEGAnno+ provides effective LLM agent and annotation management.
MEGAnno+ enables convenient and robust LLM annotation.
MEGAnno+ supports exploratory verification of LLM labels by humans.
PCC uses textual paraphrase similarity as a curriculum difficulty measure for curriculum data augmentation.
PCC includes a curriculum-aware paraphrase generation module composed of a bottom‑k sampling candidate generator, a filtering mechanism, and a difficulty measure.
PCC employs a cyclic learning strategy that traverses the curricula multiple times.
Bottom‑k sampling in PCC generates super‑hard paraphrase instances for later curriculum stages.
PCC outperforms competitive baselines on few‑shot text classification and dialogue generation tasks.

Teacher Intervention introduces a proactive knowledge distillation method that intervenes layer-wise signal propagation with teacher signals during quantization-aware training of ultra-low precision Transformers.
Teacher Intervention smooths the loss surface of quantization-aware training by removing interference from propagated quantization errors.
A gradual intervention mechanism stabilizes the recovery of Transformer layer subsections from quantization.
The proposed schemes enable fast convergence of quantization-aware training and improve model accuracy across diverse downstream fine-tuning tasks.

Teacher Intervention achieves superior accuracy with significantly fewer fine-tuning iterations on well-known Transformers for natural language processing and computer vision compared to state-of-the-art quantization-aware training methods.
State-of-the-art RST parsing architectures trained on the standard English newswire benchmark fail to generalize well within the news domain.
Genre diversity in training data significantly improves generalization of RST parsers to unseen text types.
Heterogeneous training regimes are critical for achieving stable and generalizable RST parsing across different parser architectures.
Comprehensive error analyses of RST parser outputs reveal patterns of out-of-domain performance degradation.
A full evaluation of cross-corpus RST parsing generalizability on complete trees is presented.
Between-genre degradation within an RST corpus is examined for the first time.
Impact of genre diversity in training data composition on RST parsing is investigated for the first time.
ATTEMPT inserts new concepts into taxonomies by first finding an appropriate parent node and then labeling its child nodes.
ATTEMPT leverages prompts to combine local nodes into natural sentences, enabling pre-trained language models to perform hypernym/hyponymy recognition.
ATTEMPT attains state-of-the-art results on taxonomy completion and extension tasks across multiple public datasets.
A small-scale relevant training graph is constructed per question in an unsupervised manner for answer sentence selection.
Graph neural networks are applied to the per-question training graph for answer sentence selection.
Graph nodes consist of question–answer sentence pairs.
State-of-the-art models are trained to compute relevance scores between question–question, question–answer, and answer–answer pairs, and thresholding on these scores creates graph edges.
The approach consistently outperforms state-of-the-art QA baseline models on two academic benchmarks and a real-world dataset.

Meta Self-Refinement (MSR) refines teacher pseudo-labels via meta gradient descent to maximize student performance on a clean validation set, avoiding reliance on a fixed teacher trained with noisy labels.
Meta Self-Refinement (MSR) demonstrates robustness to label noise across multiple NLP benchmarks, achieving higher accuracy and F1 than state-of-the-art methods.
FewShotTextGCN leverages word-document graph properties for improved few-shot learning on text classification.
K-hop Neighborhood Regularization is a regularizer for heterogeneous graphs that stabilizes and improves learning when only a few training samples are available.

A simplified graph-construction method produces a substantially less dense graph and yields better performance in low-resource settings while matching state-of-the-art performance in high-resource settings.
A new variant of Adaptive Pseudo-Labeling tailored for word-document graphs is introduced.
The method enables document classification without language model pretraining across typologically diverse languages and achieves performance comparable to large pretrained language models.
MiniALBERT combines model distillation and cross-layer parameter sharing to convert fully parameterized language models into compact recursive student models.
Bottleneck adapters enable layer-wise adaptation of the recursive student in MiniALBERT.
Adapter tuning can be used to fine-tune compact models derived from MiniALBERT.
MiniALBERT achieves performance comparable to state-of-the-art compact models on general and biomedical NLP tasks.
A representation and evaluation scheme for incorporating dropped pronouns into coreference resolution is proposed.
The scheme is validated on Turkish.
Annotations of the existing Turkish coreference dataset are extended to include dropped pronouns.
Public pre- and post-processing tools are provided to augment the CoNLL coreference scorer to handle coreferential relations arising from dropped pronouns.
The first neural Turkish coreference resolution results are reported.
UnifEE extracts evidence in both sentence and table cell formats using a mixed evidence graph.
The mixed evidence graph enables interactions among evidence candidates across formats at similar granularity.
Aggregating information from related evidence candidates in the fusion graph improves selection of evidence for multi-hop or combined table-text claims.
UnifEE outperforms prior evidence extraction methods.
UnifEE yields significant improvement in downstream claim verification performance.
A new publicly available dataset of 3,919 social media posts with clinically validated labels for medical self-disclosure detection is introduced.
BERTweet pretrained on social media text outperforms previous state-of-the-art models on medical self-disclosure detection.

A comparative evaluation of Transformer models pretrained on general, medical, and social media corpora reveals the impact of pretraining domain and text style on medical self-disclosure detection performance.

An empirical analysis demonstrates that existing data augmentation techniques encounter significant challenges for medical self-disclosure detection and identifies specific trends in their effectiveness.
BLM-AgrF is a French dataset for learning the underlying rules of subject-verb agreement in sentences.
BLM-AgrF is constructed within the BLM framework, a new task inspired by Raven’s Progressive Matrices.
The BLM-AgrF task requires a model to detect the generative model used to produce the dataset in order to predict the next element of a sentence sequence.
Two exploratory baselines based on commonly used architectures demonstrate that subject-verb agreement generalization is a complex problem for deep learning systems on BLM-AgrF.
Relative Position Embeddings partially encode input graph structure in AMR-to-Text generation.
The robustness of the structure-aware adapter (StructAdapt) for AMR-to-Text generation is evaluated through ablation studies, graph attacks, and link prediction.
A novel Hebrew homograph challenge set is introduced.
All existing contextualized Hebrew embedding models are evaluated on the Hebrew homograph challenge set.
Contemporary Hebrew contextualized embeddings outperform non-contextualized embeddings for homograph disambiguation.
Contemporary Hebrew contextualized embeddings are most effective for disambiguating segmentation and morphosyntactic features and less effective for pure word-sense disambiguation.
Effectiveness of Hebrew contextualized embeddings increases when the number of word-piece splits is limited.
Hebrew contextualized embeddings are more effective for 2-way and 3-way ambiguities than for 4-way ambiguities.
Hebrew contextualized embeddings are equally effective for homographs with balanced and skewed distributions, regardless of whether they are calculated as masked or unmasked tokens.
Hebrew contextualized embeddings achieve comparable effectiveness for homograph disambiguation with extensive supervised training and with few-shot setups.
Disentangled paper summarization generates separate summaries for a paper's contributions and its contextual background.
The S2ORC corpus is extended with reference labels that identify contribution and context segments of papers.
A unified model controlled by input code prefixes can produce disentangled contribution and context summaries.
A model with separate generation heads specialized for disentangled outputs can produce contribution and context summaries.
A training strategy that incorporates inbound and outbound citation supervision guides a model to generate disentangled summaries.
A comprehensive automatic evaluation protocol reports relevance, novelty, and disentanglement of generated summaries.
Expert annotators consider disentangled paper summarization more helpful than traditional scientific paper summarization.
TM-LevT integrates translation memories into the Levenshtein Transformer architecture.
Adding an extra deletion operation and modifying data presentation enables TM-LevT to achieve performance comparable to autoregressive translation while reducing decoding load.
Training with translation memories removes the necessity of knowledge distillation for mitigating multimodality in non-autoregressive machine translation.
The vanilla Levenshtein Transformer performs poorly when translating with a translation memory.
A comprehensive survey of sentence representation methods from the BERT epoch to the ChatGPT era is presented.
The survey systematically organizes the literature on sentence representation learning.
The survey highlights key contributions and challenges in sentence representation learning, focusing on deep learning models.
X-AMR annotation tool enables cross-document abstract meaning representation annotation of key corpus-level event semantics.
The tool leverages machine assistance through the Prodigy annotation platform to enhance user experience and annotation efficiency.
Empirical analyses demonstrate that the tool effectively augments an existing event corpus.
Integration of the tool with GPT-4 provides additional advantages for event annotation.
MAMMOTH is a framework for training massively multilingual modular machine translation systems at scale.
MAMMOTH adapts OpenNMT-py to enable efficient training across computation clusters.
MAMMOTH demonstrates efficient training on clusters of A100 and V100 NVIDIA GPUs.
Basic guidelines for thinking deeply about ethical issues in NLP authoring and reviewing are provided.
An interactive participatory methodology that includes case studies and group work is employed.
Participants co-build tutorial outcomes and create additional public tutorial materials.
DP-NMT is an open-source framework that enables privacy-preserving neural machine translation using differentially private stochastic gradient descent.
DP-NMT integrates multiple translation models, datasets, and evaluation metrics into a single systematic software package.
DP-NMT provides transparent and intuitive implementation details of the DP-SGD algorithm for neural machine translation.
DP-NMT is demonstrated on both general-domain and privacy-related translation datasets.
Causal interventions are employed to assess the functional relevance of encoded number information in BERT.
BERT utilizes subject plurality information only from representations of the subject and words that agree with it in number.
Current probing metrics cannot determine which words' representations contain functionally relevant information for subject-verb agreement.
Limitations of probing metrics cause current probe usage and evaluation to miss functionally relevant information.
An overview of emotion psychology research is provided to guide selection of NLP methodology for emotion analysis in texts.
Existing resources and classification methods for emotion analysis in texts are presented.
Appraisal theories and event-based emotion interpretation are discussed, and emotion role labeling is introduced.
DepressMind is an automated screening tool for analyzing social network data to monitor depression.
DepressMind extracts texts and associated meta-information from social networks and transforms them into a user‑friendly format for analysis.
DepressMind inspects user publications to establish connections between posted content and psychological dimensions defined by clinical instruments.
DepressMind associates extracted user texts with symptoms or dimensions of the Beck Depression Inventory.
Bayesian-Trans models temporal relation representations as latent variables and infers them using Bayesian inference combined with translational functions.
Bayesian-Trans infers posterior distributions over model parameters rather than point estimates, enabling explicit representation of prediction uncertainty.
Bayesian-Trans achieves higher accuracy on event temporal relation extraction than prior neural approaches.
Uncertainty quantification analysis demonstrates that Bayesian-Trans effectively encodes uncertainty about its predictions.
Prior comparison analysis shows that the choice of prior influences the performance of Bayesian-Trans.
Ablation studies reveal the contributions of Bayesian-Trans components to its overall performance.
OpenPI2.0 provides fully canonicalized entity and attribute annotations together with entity salience annotations for tracking entity state changes in text.
Evaluation on OpenPI2.0 reveals that current state-of-the-art language models perform far below competence on entity tracking.
Using state changes of salient entities as chain-of-thought prompts improves downstream performance on question answering and classical planning tasks compared to using all related entities.
A conversion framework transforms Named Entity Recognition into a seq2seq task by generating synthetic sentences using templates, enabling faster inference.
The conversion framework achieves comparable performance to existing methods in resource-rich settings.
The conversion framework outperforms the current seq2seq state-of-the-art approach in few-shot settings.
Negative examples significantly affect model performance in the conversion framework.
T5-base architecture aligns better with the conversion framework than BART.
INFUSE uses a variable premise size and simplifies summary sentences into shorter hypotheses for NLI-based faithfulness evaluation.
DiverSumm is a benchmark that includes long-form summarisation and diverse summarisation tasks such as meeting and multi-document summarisation.
An analysis of NLI-based faithfulness evaluation is performed for diverse summarisation tasks beyond single short document summarisation.
INFUSE achieves superior performance on faithfulness evaluation across multiple summarisation tasks.
ERRANT-TR is the first automatic error type classification toolkit for Turkish.
ERRANT-TR uses a rule-based error type classification pipeline that leverages word-level morphological information.
ERRANT-TR achieves strong performance on a small annotated Turkish dataset.
A hierarchical error category structure is proposed to better accommodate Turkish's inflectional and derivational morphology.
The applicability of existing grammatical error type taxonomies to Turkish is evaluated.
SaGe incorporates contextualized signal into subword vocabulary creation.
SaGe yields more cohesive token contexts than widely used tokenizers.
SaGe maintains encoding efficiency comparable to widely used tokenizers.
SaGe maintains domain robustness comparable to widely used tokenizers.
SaGe improves performance on English GLUE classification and NER tasks.
SaGe improves performance on Turkish inference and NER tasks, demonstrating robustness to morphological exponence and agglutination.
GPT-2 generated stories exhibit bias against countries with fewer internet users.
Sensitivity analysis indicates that a country's number of internet users and economic status influence the sentiment of GPT-2 generated stories.
Adversarial triggering reduces nationality bias in GPT-2 generated text.
A collection of GPT-2 generated stories for multiple nationalities is assembled to examine bias.
A dataset of crowd-sourced judgments about aspectual entailments for English verb phrases is released.
Human judgments show that aspectual class alone does not fully explain response patterns to aspectual entailment tasks.
Considering feasible accompanying scenarios of action descriptions provides a better explanation of participants' answers to aspectual entailment judgments.
GPT-3.5 outputs exhibit different patterns from human answers on aspectual entailment tasks, indicating that conceivable scenarios cannot be fully captured by language alone.
DURel implements annotation of semantic proximity between word uses via an online open-source interface.
DURel supports standardized human annotation and computational annotation using Word-in-Context models.
DURel clusters annotator judgments with automatic graph clustering techniques and visualizes the clusters for analysis.
DURel enables measurement of word senses through micro-task judgments between use pairs with minimal preparation.
DURel provides functionality to compare annotator agreement to ensure inter-subjectivity of judgments.
DURel calculates summary statistics over annotated data to reveal sense frequency distributions, semantic variation, and sense changes over time.
pyTLEX implements the TimeLine Extraction (TLEX) algorithm for Python.
pyTLEX parses TimeML annotations and constructs TimeML graphs.
pyTLEX provides a React-based visualization system for exploring temporal data and connections.
pyTLEX includes an algorithm that increases connectivity in temporal graphs by identifying disconnectivity and recommending links based on temporal reasoning.
pyTLEX incorporates a validation algorithm that checks compliance with TimeML annotation guidelines.
pyTLEX has been tested across various datasets, demonstrating its accuracy and reliability.
LLMeBench provides a framework that can be seamlessly customized to evaluate large language models for any NLP task regardless of language.
LLMeBench includes generic dataset loaders, multiple model providers, and pre-implemented standard evaluation metrics.
LLMeBench supports in-context learning with zero- and few-shot settings.
LLMeBench enables evaluation of a specific dataset and task for a given LLM with less than 20 lines of code while allowing full flexibility to extend to custom datasets, models, or tasks.
LLMeBench has been empirically tested across dozens of NLP tasks and publicly available datasets in many experimental setups.
Scene-dialogue alignment is introduced as a method for multimodal object identification.
Scene-dialogue alignment improves multimodal object identification performance on the SIMMC 2.1 dataset relative to existing baselines.
Three multimodal object identification methods are evaluated on the SIMMC 2.1 dataset.
An analysis of the limitations of the evaluated multimodal object identification methods is provided.
Portuguese contact center dialog datasets are created.
An extraction component processes dialogs to generate descriptive interaction information.
A representation component discovers frequent dialog flows and represents them as graphs.
A guidance component assists agents during new dialogs.
A unified framework integrates extraction, representation, and guidance components for automatic dialog flow extraction and agent assistance.
Similarity-sensitive Shannon entropy (S3E) is adapted for neural machine translation.
S3E shows improved correlation with task performance on quality estimation and named entity recall.
S3E provides a useful framework for measuring uncertainty in neural machine translation.
Logic-Explainer integrates large language models with a backward-chaining solver to iteratively refine step-wise natural language explanations.
Logic-Explainer jointly verifies the correctness of refined explanations, reducing incompleteness and redundancy.
Logic-Explainer improves explanations generated by in-context learning and Chain-of-Thought methods on challenging ethical natural language inference tasks.
Logic-Explainer produces formal proofs that describe and support the reasoning of large language models.
Empirical analysis shows that neuro-symbolic methods enhance logical consistency, reliability, and alignment of large language models for multi-step natural language inference.
An Instruction-tuning-CoT method aligns and transfers chain-of-thought reasoning abilities from larger to smaller language models.
The Instruction-tuning-CoT method enables small language models to generate multi-step controlled reasoned answers when prompted with chain-of-thought.
Instruction-tuned small language models outperform baseline models on question-answering and mathematical reasoning benchmarks in both in-domain and out-of-domain settings.
The impact of the Instruction-tuning-CoT method is evaluated across different types of language models.

A systematic analysis of the impact of various anonymization techniques on the performance of state-of-the-art machine learning models across five NLP tasks is conducted using multiple clinical datasets.
Anonymization techniques affect the performance of state-of-the-art machine learning models differently across five NLP tasks.
A simple re-identification attack can break the anonymization of clinical text data.
Insights and recommendations for text anonymization are derived based on observed performance impacts on machine learning models.
TextBI is a multimodal generic dashboard that visualizes multidimensional text annotations on large volumes of multilingual social media data.
TextBI visualizes annotations across spatial, temporal, thematic, and personal dimensions and supports additional enrichment data such as sentiment and engagement.
TextBI provides visualization modes including frequency, movement, and association.
TextBI offers an interactive interface tailored for domain stakeholders and NLP researchers to interpret NLP annotations.
Experiments in the tourism domain using X data demonstrate the applicability of TextBI for tourism offices.
TextBI integrates features from Business Intelligence, Geographical Information Systems, and NLP tools to advance visualization of NLP annotations.
Kardeş-NLU provides language understanding datasets for Azerbaijani, Kazakh, Kyrgyz, Uzbek, and Uyghur.
Integrating Turkish in intermediate training yields substantial improvements in cross-lingual transfer to low-resource Turkic languages.
Integrating Turkish in downstream fine-tuning yields substantial improvements in cross-lingual transfer to low-resource Turkic languages.
Instruction-tuned large language models exhibit highly task- and language-dependent performance on Kardeş-NLU.
A transformation module converts infrequent candidate mention representations during evaluation into the average mention representation computed from the training dataset.
The framework improves extraction performance on rare entity and event types across three benchmark datasets.
The ARI social robot dialogue system receives speech and video inputs and generates speech and gestures (arm, head, and eye movements) for multi-party conversations in a hospital memory clinic.
The ARI system decides when to take its turn in multi-party spoken interactions.
The ARI system generates human-like clarification requests when a patient pauses mid-utterance.
The ARI system answers in-domain questions by grounding to the in-prompt knowledge.
The ARI system responds to out-of-domain requests by generating jokes or quizzes, handling unexpected patient utterances.
Recent unsupervised stance detection baselines have limited generalization across heterophilic and homophilic social media networks.
A baseline model that uses text embeddings propagated with a graph neural network generalizes well to both heterophilic and homophilic networks.
The baseline model outperforms other state-of-the-art unsupervised stance detection methods on average across heterophilic and homophilic networks.
Combining textual and network information yields better stance detection performance than using text only.
Language model size has limited impact on unsupervised stance detection performance.

A large-scale study varying auxiliary target-side languages by linguistic similarity and corpus size reveals the dynamic impact of knowledge transfer on main language pairs in multilingual machine translation.
Linguistically similar auxiliary target languages provide strong positive knowledge transfer to main language pairs.
Increasing the amount of similar auxiliary target language data further enhances positive transfer to main language pairs.
Distant auxiliary target languages can unexpectedly benefit main language pairs despite minimal positive transfer ability.
Distant auxiliary target languages act as a regularizer that improves translation performance by enhancing model generalization and inference calibration.
Combining the Correspondence Auto-Encoder with self-supervised speech representations improves acoustic word embeddings.
A HuBERT-based Correspondence Auto-Encoder achieves the best word discrimination performance among evaluated models across Polish, Portuguese, Spanish, French, and English.

The HuBERT-based Correspondence Auto-Encoder provides effective cross-lingual acoustic word embeddings, outperforming MFCC-based CAE models when trained on a source language and tested on target languages.
A method measures the impact of each audio segment aligned with a word on speech classification outcomes.
The method evaluates how non-linguistic features such as prosody and background noise affect speech classification outcomes when perturbed.
The method is validated by explaining two state-of-the-art spoken language understanding models on tasks in English and Italian.
Human subject ratings indicate that the explanations correctly represent the models' inner workings.
Human subject ratings indicate that the explanations are plausible to humans.
A framework iteratively trains dense retrievers to identify high-quality in-context examples for large language models.
The framework trains a reward model using large language model feedback to evaluate the quality of candidate examples.
The framework employs knowledge distillation to train a bi-encoder dense retriever.
The framework enhances in-context learning performance across a diverse set of tasks.
The framework generalizes to unseen tasks that were not present during training.
Retrieving examples with similar patterns improves in-context learning performance.
Performance gains from the framework are consistent across large language models of varying sizes.
Analogy Detection and Extraction (AnaDE) is defined as a task comprising document-level analogy detection, analogy segment extraction, and source/target concept identification.
A benchmark dataset for AnaDE is created by scraping Metamia.com.
State-of-the-art models are evaluated on all three AnaDE sub-tasks, establishing first-generation benchmark results.
Longformer achieves the best performance across all three AnaDE sub-tasks, demonstrating effectiveness for handling long texts.
Smaller models fine-tuned on the AnaDE dataset outperform non-fine-tuned ChatGPT, indicating high task difficulty.
Models achieve high performance on document detection, suggesting feasibility for analogy search engine applications.
Cross-modal selective self-training (CMSST) enables zero-shot end-to-end spoken language understanding using only speech-text and text-semantics pairs.
CMSST addresses data imbalance by clustering speech, text, and semantics representations in a joint space.
CMSST mitigates label noise through a selection network.
Two benchmarks for zero-shot end-to-end spoken language understanding are introduced, covering matched and mismatched speech-text and text-semantics domain settings.
CMSST improves performance in both benchmark settings while requiring fewer training samples and less training time.
A neuron-level analysis of multilingual BERT is performed using the multilingual factual knowledge probing dataset mLAMA.
A method is introduced to trace facts encoded in multilingual language models back to their Wikipedia source.
Three patterns of factual knowledge acquisition in multilingual language models are identified: language-independent, cross-lingual shared, and transferred.
Methods for differentiating language-independent, cross-lingual shared, and transferred factual knowledge patterns in multilingual language models are devised.
Empirical findings indicate that maintaining consistent factual knowledge across languages is challenging for multilingual language models.
μPLAN performs cross-lingual summarization using an intermediate planning step as a cross-lingual bridge.

The planning step formulates a sequence of entities that capture summary content and order, abstracted from surface form via a multilingual knowledge base aligning entities to canonical designations across languages.
Summary generation is conditioned on the cross-lingual bridge and the input document.
The planning objective achieves state-of-the-art informativeness and faithfulness on the XWikis dataset across four language pairs.
μPLAN improves zero-shot transfer to new cross-lingual language pairs compared to baselines without a planning component.
An iterative-prompting-based planning method grounded in cognitive psychology and narratology generates suspenseful stories with large language models.
The method operates in a fully zero-shot manner without requiring supervised story corpora.
Human evaluations indicate that the method produces suspenseful stories.
This work constitutes the first attempt at suspenseful story generation using large language models.
DocChecker detects inconsistencies between code and comments using a language model-based framework.
DocChecker generates synthetic comments to resolve code-comment inconsistencies.
DocChecker establishes a new benchmark on the Inconsistency Code-Comment Detection task.
DocChecker achieves state-of-the-art performance on code summarization.
DocChecker outperforms GPT-3.5 and CodeLlama on code-comment inconsistency detection and code summarization.
A framework of agency features expressed in dialogue, including Intentionality, Motivation, Self-Efficacy, and Self-Regulation, is developed.
A dataset of human-human collaborative interior design conversations annotated for agency features is collected.
Methods for measuring agency of large language models are developed based on the annotated dataset.
Automatic and human evaluations show that large language models manifesting high Intentionality, Motivation, Self-Efficacy, and Self-Regulation are perceived as strongly agentive.
Methods process English-language podcast transcripts to extract narrative content from episode conversations.
An initial analysis characterizes the types of narrative content present across a wide range of podcasts.
Results are compared with other established narrative analysis tools.
Annotations for narrativity and pretrained models are released to support future research on narrativity in a corpus of approximately 100,000 podcast episodes.
STable is a framework for text-to-table generation with encoder-decoder models.
STable employs a permutation-based decoder that comprehends information from all cells in a table.
STable training maximizes the expected log-likelihood of a table's content across random permutations of the factorization order.
STable inference searches over possible cell generation orderings to maximize model confidence and reduce error accumulation.
STable achieves state-of-the-art performance on several challenging text-to-table datasets.
LMCor merges candidate outputs generated by a large language model to produce an enhanced output.
LMCor improves the few-shot performance of large language models without requiring fine-tuning.
LMCor matches or outperforms standard fine-tuning of large language models on natural language generation tasks.
LMCor is robust to different prompts, reducing the need for extensive prompt engineering.
LMCor can be integrated with various large language models at inference as a plug-and-play module.
Three aspects of diversity in argument summarization are defined: opinions, annotators, and sources.

Existing argument summarization approaches for Key Point Analysis struggle to represent arguments shared by few people, handle data from various sources, and align with subjectivity in human annotations.
Both general-purpose large language models and dedicated Key Point Analysis models exhibit difficulties with diversity aspects but possess complementary strengths.
Diversifying training data improves zero-shot generalization in argument summarization.
A2R framework defines a three-stage process (Ask, Assess, Refine) for improving factual consistency in large language model responses.
A2R framework incorporates metrics specifically designed to assess citation errors and hallucination in large language model outputs.
A2R framework generates actionable natural language feedback to iteratively refine large language model responses.
A2R framework enhances correctness, fluency, and citation quality on the ASQA, ELI5, and QAMPARI datasets.
An extensive evaluation of six Neural Theory-of-Mind tasks assesses large language models' N-ToM abilities.
Large language models exhibit some Neural Theory-of-Mind abilities but these abilities are not robust.
Large language models struggle with adversarial examples on Neural Theory-of-Mind tasks, indicating reliance on shallow heuristics rather than robust Theory-of-Mind abilities.
A graph-guided mechanism automatically generates exhaustive and controllable question-answer pairs from procedural text.
The mechanism represents each step and the overall flow of a procedure as graphs and conditions on graph nodes to generate QA pairs.
Training compact QA models on the generated data yields performance competitive with, and sometimes exceeding, GPT-3 and ChatGPT despite being orders of magnitude smaller.
Semantic coverage of generated training data is identified as a key predictor of downstream QA performance.
Higher semantic coverage, rather than syntactic diversity, is critical for improving QA performance.
Counterfactual Contrastive Prefix-tuning is a prefix-tuning method designed for many-class classification.
Counterfactual Contrastive Prefix-tuning leverages an instance-dependent soft prefix derived from fact‑counterfactual pairs to complement language verbalizers.
Counterfactual Contrastive Prefix-tuning outperforms prior baselines on many-class benchmark datasets in both fully supervised and few‑shot settings.
GAP-Gen generates Python code guided by Python syntactic constraints called Syntax-Flow.
Syntax-Flow is a simplified representation of the abstract syntax tree that reduces size and complexity while preserving essential syntactic information of Python code.
Variable-Flow abstracts variable and function names consistently throughout generated code.
GAP-Gen modifies the fine-tuning process of transformer-based language models to reduce computational requirements while retaining high generation performance for Python code generation.
GAP-Gen fine-tunes T5 and CodeT5 models using the CodeSearchNet, CodeSearchNet AdvTest, and Code-Docstring Corpus datasets.
GAP-Gen achieves superior performance on automatic Python code generation compared to prior methods.
MIMOSA is a Bengali multimodal aggression dataset comprising 4,848 annotated memes labeled with five target categories: Political, Gender, Religious, Others, and non‑aggressive.
Multimodal Attentive Fusion (MAF) detects aggression targets in memes by capturing selective modality‑specific features and jointly evaluating them with individual modality features.
MAF achieves higher performance than several state‑of‑the‑art approaches on the MIMOSA dataset.
A contrastive event aggregation network constructs adaptively-updated semantic representations for triggers and arguments to introduce event knowledge into supervised learning.
A new LLM-based augmentation scheme combines a multi-pattern rephrasing paradigm with a data-free composing paradigm.
Span-level contrastive learning is employed to reduce data noise introduced by augmentation samples.
The proposed approach attains new state-of-the-art performance on the ACE2005 and ERE-EN event extraction datasets.
A comparative analysis of visual representations in vision-and-language models and vision-only models is performed using probing tasks.
Five probing tasks are evaluated to assess the quality of learned visual representations.
Vision-and-language models achieve higher accuracy on label prediction tasks such as object and attribute prediction than vision-only models.
Vision-only models achieve higher accuracy on dense prediction tasks that require localized information than vision-and-language models.
Skeleton-Assisted Prompt Transfer (SAPT) leverages skeleton generation as extra supervision to connect dialogue state tracking and dialogue summarization in prompt tuning.
A perturbation-based probing approach automatically extracts dialogue skeletons for supervised training without annotation effort or domain knowledge.
Training on automatically extracted skeletons preserves model capability during prompt transfer.
SAPT improves performance over existing baselines in few-shot dialogue summarization.
Analyses demonstrate that SAPT facilitates cross-task knowledge transfer in few-shot dialogue summarization.
NevIR provides a benchmark that asks information retrieval models to rank two documents that differ only by negation.
Cross-encoders achieve the highest performance on the negation benchmark among evaluated neural IR architectures.
Late-interaction models outperform bi-encoders and sparse neural architectures on the negation benchmark.
Most current information retrieval models rank documents with negation similarly to random ranking, indicating they do not consider negation.
Continued fine-tuning on a contrastive dataset containing negations improves IR model performance on the benchmark, yet a large gap remains between machine and human performance.
A malware behavior search technique leverages graph isomorphism on attention layers of Transformer models.
A large dataset of malware behavior reports collected from various agencies is compiled to support malware behavior search research.
The graph-isomorphism-based search technique outperforms state-of-the-art methods that use sentence embeddings and keyword matching.
The graph-isomorphism-based search technique attributes real-world malware to their ground truth origins more successfully than Google search.
End-user specification-aware document-to-slides conversion incorporates end-user specifications into the conversion process.
A new dataset derived from SciDuet provides four persona-aware configurations for document-to-slides conversion.
Persona-Aware-D2S fine-tunes large language models with target audience feedback to generate persona-aware slides from scientific documents.
Incorporating end-user specifications enables generation of informative presentations tailored to the expectations and cognitive abilities of the target audience.
Counterfactually-Fair-Prompt (CFP) method is introduced for fairness-aware LLM-based recommendation.
Unbiased Foundation Models (UFO) framework is introduced for fairness-aware LLM-based recommendation.
Unfairness of LLM-based recommender models built on T5 and LLaMA backbones is empirically analyzed.

CFP achieves better recommendation performance while maintaining a high level of fairness compared to matching-based and sequential-based fairness-aware recommendation models on MovieLens-1M and Insurance datasets.
Sig-Networks is an open-source, pip-installable toolkit for longitudinal language modelling.
Sig-Networks provides signature-based neural network models as reusable PyTorch building blocks.
Sig-Networks enables task-agnostic dataset plug-in, seamless sequential data preprocessing, parameter flexibility, and automated tuning across a range of models.
Signature networks implemented with Sig-Networks achieve state-of-the-art performance on counselling conversations, rumor stance switching, and mood change detection in social media threads.
Archer is a data-augmentation strategy that exploits alignment annotations between sentences and their corresponding meaning representations.
Archer trains a two-step generative model that combines monotonic lexical generation with reordering.
Archer improves compositional generalization performance.
Systematic experiments evaluate open-domain claim verification performance across four biomedical and health claim datasets while varying knowledge sources and information retrieval techniques.
